programme.title: Comanche Station (1960)
programme.desc: A cowboy recruits a trio of outlaws to rescue a woman held captive at a Comanche camp and reunite her with her husband in return for a large reward. However, his partners decide the bounty should be split three ways rather than four and plan to double-cross him. Western, starring Randolph Scott, Nancy Gates, Claude Akins and Skip Homeier

programme.title: Silverado (1985)
programme.desc: Two drifters meet by chance in the desert, later joined on their travels by one of the duo's reckless younger brothers and a man they break out of prison. The mismatched foursome rides to a run-down frontier town and fight to free it from the control of a crooked marshal and his cronies. Western, starring Kevin Kline, Kevin Costner, Scott Glenn, Danny Glover, Rosanna Arquette and Jeff Goldblum

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.desc: A strange virus causes Kirk, Scotty and Dr McCoy to age rapidly, and it seems their days are numbered unless they can find a cure. Sci-fi adventure, starring William Shatner and Leonard Nimoy

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.sub-title: The Return of the Bionic Woman _ Part One
programme.desc: Part one. Steve is understandably shocked to discover his supposedly dead fiancee is actually alive and kicking

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: A robbery hostage loses her unborn child during a car chase, focusing hostile media attention on the department's high-speed pursuit policy. American police drama, starring William Shatner

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The lawman tracks down a hijacked ambulance containing a donor organ. Dionne Warwick guest stars

programme.title: Hunter Killer (2018)
programme.desc: Unconventional submarine skipper Joe Glass is sent to investigate the torpedoing of US and Russian vessels. He winds up trying to rescue the Russian president, who has been kidnapped by a rogue general. Action thriller, starring Gerard Butler, Gary Oldman and Common

programme.title: Faster (2010)
programme.desc: A criminal is released from prison with revenge in mind. His brother was murdered while he was behind bars. He hunts down the men responsible, pursued by a cop on the verge of retirement and a ruthless hitman. Action adventure, starring Dwayne Johnson, Billy Bob Thornton, Oliver Jackson-Cohen and Carla Gugino

programme.title: Centurion (2010)
programme.desc: A Roman legion is sent on a mission to crush a Pictish uprising, only to be nearly wiped out in a savage ambush. A centurion who survives the massacre takes charge of the survivors and leads them on a daring mission to rescue their captured general and make it back to their own territory alive. Neil Marshall's action thriller, with Michael Fassbender, Dominic West and Olga Kurylenko

programme.title: Escape Plan 3: The Extractors (2019)
programme.desc: After a couple of previous high stakes assignments, security expert Ray Breslin is hired to rescue the kidnapped daughter of a Hong Kong tech mogul from a formidable Latvian prison. Problems continues to arise when Breslin's girlfriend is also captured. Director John Herzfeld's action thriller sequel, starring Sylvester Stallone, Dave Bautista and 50 Cent

programme.title: Deadly Target (2015)
programme.desc: A soldier returning home from duty overseas witnesses his brother's murder, and vows to get revenge. As he pursues the killers, he uncovers a secret conspiracy within the US government and must fight for survival against a ruthless assassin sent to stop him revealing what he knows. Action thriller, starring Michael Jai White and Steve Austin

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A teenager has a life-threatening bleed on the brain and requires emergency surgery, a 10-year-old gymnast sustains an injury to her wrist after performing a back bend, and a girl is brought in to the urgent care centre, needing treatment for her swollen tonsils. As they undergo treatment, their parents share stories of family life

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A young man is found drunk and passed out in the street, pregnant woman is vomiting continuously and exhausted, while a man is in agony with what the paramedics think is gallstones. An elderly man falls over and badly cuts his head. He lives alone with help from carers and is fiercely independent, but his son is concerned this will happen again

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A patient undergoing treatment for cancer develops a swelling in her throat that doctors fear may be a side-effect of the revolutionary new procedure she has been undergoing. Drastic steps have to be taken to save a patient with a dangerously high heart rate, and a seven-year-old boy is brought in after injuring his chest in a golf buggy accident

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A woman is transferred to St George's after crashing her car into a tree, and has to have her broken ankle very quickly realigned if she is to have any hope of walking again. A man who collapsed suddenly is suspected of having a life-threatening head injury, while a four-year-old girl needs treatment after falling on an escalator

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: Midway through their tour of the Scottish Highlands, Sean and Marie, Jen, Ian and Ellis, and Simon and Emmy pitch up at Ben Nevis Holiday Park, at the foot of the famous landmark

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Guildford, Surrey, the first host is glamorous 22-year-old cosmetic-surgery-fan Shanice, who hopes to show she is more than just a pretty face by serving up sumptuous food and dazzling conversation. The guests she must impress are 32-year-old entertainer David, 69-year-old Norwegian plain-talker Vivi, 47-year-old super-competitive Neil and 25-year-old media sales worker Charlotte. With risky menu choices and a fellow diner's peculiar laughter taking the spotlight, will the conversations ranging from lookalikes to riding polar bears be enough to secure Shanice the prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Retired estate agent Vivi hosts the second dinner party in and around Guildford. She is convinced her experience and original Scandinavian fayre can beat her less experienced competition and the soiree features Norwegian fish soup, ghost stories, ghost impressions, Norwegian singing and nursery rhymes

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sales manager Charlotte hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in and around Guildford, hoping to impress her guests enough to win the £1,000 prize money. However, she has taken a huge risk with an Asian-inspired menu, which could prove to be too exotic for entertainments manager David

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The penultimate day in and around Guildford, Surrey, sees children's entertainments manager David playing host. Having worked in the entertainments industry all his life, he is no stranger to showing people a good time, and employs his colleagues to put on a performance, while keeping his food simple. Is he forgetting the key ingredients of a dinner party?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final day in and around Guildford, Surrey, sales director Neil devises a sophisticated menu, dismissing his predecessors' efforts as unambitious. However, his unusual fayre is tempered by his choice of yodelling entertainment, which threatens to hinder his chance of scooping the £1,000 prize

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lee Juggurnauth jets off to the historic town of Mojácar in Spain's Almería region to help house-hunters Christine and Howard find the perfect holiday home. After 30 years of marriage and a few difficult years back at home, the couple have decided it's finally the right time to fulfil their long-held dream of buying a place in the sun. Lee sources five properties within their £50,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sports fan and rugby coach Matt loves all-things Italian and dreams of buying a holiday home near the historic city of Lucca in Tuscany. He's looking for a property with two bedrooms in a lively location, and brings along his best friend Shaun for some straight-talking advice. Jean Johansson secures the options of five properties for him to vivist within his £190,000 budget

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help the Clayton-Fishers, who have fallen out of love with their home in Walthamstow, east London. Sarah and her wife Pepper have opposing views on what to do with their two-bedroom terrace, as Sarah thinks it is time to move on, but Pepper is digging her heels in and wants to convert the loft

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: In the seaside town of Weymouth, Dorset, the presenter helps a couple whose first-time family home was destroyed overnight by a flash flood. When they bought their 19th-century coach house in 2018, they had planned to renovate its higgledy-piggledy layout, but the overnight disaster means they've got a far bigger project than they bargained for. The architect also visits Chiswick, west London, to assist a couple whose master bedroom and bathroom he redesigned a couple of years ago. With a growing family, next on their demolition list is the pokey kitchen

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jean Johansson heads to the glorious Greek island of Crete to help Jeanette and Phil, who are dreaming of starting a new, relaxed way of life in the sunshine while spending more quality time together. They're focusing their search around the beautiful, ancient city of Chania and ideally want a three-bedroom detached house with a pool. They have allocated a budget of £170,000, so Jean hunts out five properties for them to consider

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Yvette and Agata call upon the services of Jasmine Harman in their hunt for their dream three-bed 'forever home' in the seaside town of Alvor on Portugal's Algarve. Property expert Jasmine rustles up five options for their budget of £300,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton goes house-hunting around the Messinia Peninsula in Greece with Martin and Sonia from Bristol. The couple have been travelling extensively around Greece for many years since their first trip 36 years ago, and now are dreaming of retiring there permanently. Utilising their maximum budget of £125,000, Laura lines up five options to show them, hopeful one will prove to be the pair's 'forever home'

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week kicks off at The Earlsdale in Devon, where married couple Lewis and James hope their bold sense of style will prove a hit, although a dribbly shower rains on their chances

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: In Bude in Cornwall, the B&Bers visit The Kings Arms, where hosts Sally and Jo set out to give their guests the royal treatment. During the last visit, the pair were bullish about their spotless cleanliness standards - so, naturally, that's the first thing all of their guests check as soon as they arrive. And it comes back to bite Sally and Jo at feedback time, as they accuse some of their rivals of tit-for-tat marking

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: In Dorset, newbie B&Ber Hilary aims to provide a home-from-home at her East Creek B&B - which doubles as her actual family home. The stunning coastal views instantly earn Hilary some brownie points, but a lavatory let-down leaves two guests with a dilemma. After a visit to a sculpture park, Hilary has an awkward moment dealing with a complaint, leading to some low scores in her feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to The Railway Ramblers' Rest in Devon, where hosts Nikki and Terry hope their unique take on self-catering will secure them the plaque. However, simmering tensions between two of their guests threaten to derail things - and a simple breakfast the next morning isn't to everyone's taste

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&Bers meet up on payment day to settle some scores and find out how much they've been paid. Cleanliness concerns are at the top of the agenda for most of the hosts, after one pair gave consistently low scores throughout the week. Allegations of foul play get the blood boiling, before the little pink envelopes come out and the identity of the winner is revealed

programme.title: The Emergency Ward
programme.desc: Dr Elyssia and Dr Elliott examine a 51-year-old rock climber who has life-threatening injuries after falling seven metres. Meanwhile, Dr Martin prepares a specialised team to help a 29-year-old motorbike rider who's been in a terrible accident and has extensive head, chest, neck and pelvic injuries, and only minutes to survive. And Dr Michelle fears for an equestrian whose spooked horse kicked her

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 92-year-old man may have a bleed on the brain after falling and hitting his head, so is sent for a CT scan. Two patients who came to the UK from Iran are brought in - one suffering from chest pains, the other having difficulty speaking. They and their friends and family share their memories of the revolution and how it affected their lives

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics treat a woman who's fallen headfirst through a glass door, leaving a deep wound across her head, and a man complaining of crushing headaches who has a family history of brain haemorrhages. They also attend a car that's embedded in a garden wall after a two-vehicle smash and are called out to a drunk man who's been found collapsed in the street and is clutching his chest. The paramedics turn detectives to try to figure out why a woman keeps passing out

programme.title: The Emergency Ward
programme.desc: Dr Elyssia and Dr Elliott examine a 51-year-old rock climber who has life-threatening injuries after falling seven metres. Meanwhile, Dr Martin prepares a specialised team to help a 29-year-old motorbike rider who's been in a terrible accident and has extensive head, chest, neck and pelvic injuries, and only minutes to survive. And Dr Michelle fears for an equestrian whose spooked horse kicked her

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 92-year-old man may have a bleed on the brain after falling and hitting his head, so is sent for a CT scan. Two patients who came to the UK from Iran are brought in - one suffering from chest pains, the other having difficulty speaking. They and their friends and family share their memories of the revolution and how it affected their lives

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A teenager has a life-threatening bleed on the brain and requires emergency surgery, a 10-year-old gymnast sustains an injury to her wrist after performing a back bend, and a girl is brought in to the urgent care centre, needing treatment for her swollen tonsils. As they undergo treatment, their parents share stories of family life

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: This week's campers arrive at Dalraddy Holiday Park on a traditional Scottish estate at Aviemore. Without leaving the site, campers Jen, Ian and Ellis, Sean and Marie, and Emmy and Simon take a train ride, enjoy some quirky Highland games and battle it out on the paintballing field

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Hay-on-Wye and Bedford. Henry lovingly restores a bespoke workbench and Simon ponders the possibilities of some discarded planks of wood

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien restores an enamel roll-top bath and builds a den out of old doors. Henry Cole makes a wall mounted safe from a jerry can and lovingly restores a cheese-making trough

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Brigitte Tee, who runs four-star B&B Gorse Meadow in the New Forest, hosts the first round of the competition. Her guests are initially charmed by her eccentric personality, but the discovery of a bathroom window in need of repair and another room containing ladybirds, cobwebs and dust soon takes the shine off their stay

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants head to Lynton in north Devon, where they stay at Alistair and Emma Mundell's four-star Castle Hill Guesthouse. The pair only opened the B&B three months prior to the competition, and hope to impress with their recently renovated rooms, but Brigitte Tee proves hard to win over. Two of their guests are also forced to overcome their fears during a trip on a Victorian cliff railway

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves north to Blackpool and the three-star Vidella B&B run by John Downer and Neil Williams. The pair rig Blackpool-hater Alistair Mundell's room with practical jokes, and take their guests to Funny Girls drag cabaret bar, where Mark Crib confesses he has never had such a surreal time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Entrepreneur Mark Crib welcomes the group to the Urban Beach in Bournemouth, Dorset, for the week's final B&B visit. The boutique establishment features chic rooms with deluxe bathrooms, and the guests approve of the afternoon's activity - a speedboat ride along the Jurassic coast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. Brigitte Tee is upset to discover she has been given only one point for cleanliness and a heated exchange results in a walk-out, before the winner is announced

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the first night in Peterborough, 39-year-old land surveyor and part-time musician Muneer hopes to win over his guests with a menu based on Pakistani recipes passed down through his family for generations. The authenticity extends beyond his food, with Muneer seating his guests on the floor of his specially dressed dining room. His guests include driving instructor Christine, hairdresser Ben, fiery Brazilian Rosie and fridge salesman Gregory. While his food receives mixed reviews, His guests discover his master plan for musical success, suffer bad jokes and find out that one of their fellow diners believes in mermaids

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Peterborough, driving instructor Christine hopes her vegetarian cuisine will grab her the £1,000 prize. But she's worried about being in the firing line, after Rosie compared Muneer's food to vomit on the first night. Now Moony's determined to give Rosie a public dressing down. However, with everyone dreading Christine's meat-free and bland-sounding menu, Rosie may not be the only one who has to bite her lip

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Peterborough, mobile hairdresser Ben plans a gourmet night. Most of his guests are looking forward to some meat after Christine's vegetarian feast the night before. And after laid-back Muneer and critical Rosie seem to have put their tension behind them at Christine's, Ben's determined to take things up a notch as he entertains his guests with his amazing body art, cooking skills and pet crabs

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fridge salesman Gregory serves up a Spanish menu in Peterborough, accompanied by a charm offensive that he hopes will win him the £1,000 prize. Will it work or will the guests give him the cold shoulder?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fiery Brazilian Rosie is confident that her night will be the best of the week in Peterborough. After Gregory used packet mixes, Rosie is determined to show him how to throw a dinner party by making everything from scratch. She's ruffled a few feathers throughout the week with her brutally honest critiques, and her guests, roused for retribution, scour every element of her Brazilian night for any faults, from her samba dancers to her authentic pig-ear stew

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at stately home Anstey Hall in Cambridge, with father and son team John and Johnny de Bruyne. The fun starts with a character-revealing afternoon of goose-herding, before the participants dress to impress at a formal dinner. John recounts stories of royal connections - which fails to impress hotel managers Mark and Layla. In the morning, as they tuck in to a self-service breakfast, guest Tim decides that DIY doesn't work at Anstey Hall, but when he notes this on his feedback form, John and Johnny accuse him of game-playing

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the competition is to clothes-free Clover Spa and Hotel in Birmingham, where owner Tim Higgs hopes to convert his rivals to the joys of naturism. Judging with an extra critical eye are John and Jonny De Bruyne, who received negative feedback from Tim, and a thorough room inspection reveals a dusty porn DVD. Tim tries to encourage his nervous guests to take part in a naked spa experience, but John opts to watch television instead. When feedback is invited, Tim is delighted to discover Mark and Layla would stay again but shocked by the 'porn film' discovery

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the Angel Hotel in Coleford, Gloucestershire for the third day of the competition, where a treasure hunt organised by hosts Mark and Layla brings out their guests' competitive sides. Meanwhile, there is confusion at breakfast and Layla's manners are criticised

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at Higher Gitcombe in Totnes, Devon, where owners Paul and Lynnie Jolly set out to spoil their guests. Everyone is overwhelmed by the added extras, but naturist Tim is put off by cushions and clutter. After a masterclass in Nordic walking, Lynnie serves up a veg smoothie, which unhealthy eaters Mark and Layla dubiously down in one. Over dinner, Tim challenges his host's organic 'niche'. And Layla, still smarting from his negative feedback, labels him 'weird, naked Tim'. At breakfast, guests are wary as Tim's order goes wrong and Jonny and Layla engage in an eating competition. When feedback is invited, the Jollys are affronted by accusations of special treatment and vow to put the record straight

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On the final day of the competition, the owners of Anstey Hall, the Clover Spa and Hotel, the Angel Hotel and Higher Gitcombe meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and settle some scores. Having aired their issues, the winners are announced

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer search for properties in south and east London. Sisters Ruth and Anna are pooling funds, while Aba, also a first-time buyer, confesses to champagne tastes, but lemonade money

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts as five more players go head-to-head in the second heat of six rounds. Who'll get the highest score to secure a place in the Grand Final?

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 75-year-old man who fell out of bed is suspected of having a stroke, and doctors have only a short window of time to administer a life-saving, clot-busting drug. A mother of two falls off her horse, and a six-year-old girl has a possible neck injury after an accident in the playground. Family members of the patients share their personal stories

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Mark, 32, is brought to resus with a head injury after having an epileptic seizure, and he is sent for an urgent CT scan to assess the damage to his brain. Anastasia, 80, comes to urgent care with a pain in her leg and while she is sent for an X-ray to rule out a blood clot, she shares the story of her arrival to study in England from Nigeria, only for civil war to break out back home. Porter Chris, who's been part of the A&E department for 25 years, shares his insights into the job as he goes about his day

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: The paramedics treat a cafe worker struggling to speak, having possibly suffered a stroke. A woman who's in the process of giving birth to her seventh child hasn't felt the baby move in over 10 hours and there are signs the baby could be in distress. Another pregnant woman and her toddler son are involved in a car crash, and a patient is found collapsed in a car park

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: The East Anglian Air Ambulance crew fight to save their patient's partially amputated hand in Stowmarket and a crew in High Wycombe to help a man in cardiac arrest. The Essex and Herts Air Ambulance team deal with Becky's badly broken ankle in Chelmsford and in Hatfield, a motorcyclist suffers multiple injuries after a collision with a van

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 75-year-old man who fell out of bed is suspected of having a stroke, and doctors have only a short window of time to administer a life-saving, clot-busting drug. A mother of two falls off her horse, and a six-year-old girl has a possible neck injury after an accident in the playground. Family members of the patients share their personal stories

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Mark, 32, is brought to resus with a head injury after having an epileptic seizure, and he is sent for an urgent CT scan to assess the damage to his brain. Anastasia, 80, comes to urgent care with a pain in her leg and while she is sent for an X-ray to rule out a blood clot, she shares the story of her arrival to study in England from Nigeria, only for civil war to break out back home. Porter Chris, who's been part of the A&E department for 25 years, shares his insights into the job as he goes about his day

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: With their tour of the Highlands drawing to a close, the campers head for Scotland's northern coast, pitching up at Barrow Campsite near Nairn. Having visited five campsites, it's time for Sean and Marie, Simon and Emmy, and Ian, Jen and Ellis to pick this week's perfect pitch. But first - there's some paddleboarding, beach yoga and campfire cocktails to enjoy

programme.title: The Emergency Ward
programme.desc: Dr Elyssia and Dr Elliott examine a 51-year-old rock climber who has life-threatening injuries after falling seven metres. Meanwhile, Dr Martin prepares a specialised team to help a 29-year-old motorbike rider who's been in a terrible accident and has extensive head, chest, neck and pelvic injuries, and only minutes to survive. And Dr Michelle fears for an equestrian whose spooked horse kicked her

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A patient undergoing treatment for cancer develops a swelling in her throat that doctors fear may be a side-effect of the revolutionary new procedure she has been undergoing. Drastic steps have to be taken to save a patient with a dangerously high heart rate, and a seven-year-old boy is brought in after injuring his chest in a golf buggy accident

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A woman is transferred to St George's after crashing her car into a tree, and has to have her broken ankle very quickly realigned if she is to have any hope of walking again. A man who collapsed suddenly is suspected of having a life-threatening head injury, while a four-year-old girl needs treatment after falling on an escalator

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: Midway through their tour of the Scottish Highlands, Sean and Marie, Jen, Ian and Ellis, and Simon and Emmy pitch up at Ben Nevis Holiday Park, at the foot of the famous landmark

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Guildford, Surrey, the first host is glamorous 22-year-old cosmetic-surgery-fan Shanice, who hopes to show she is more than just a pretty face by serving up sumptuous food and dazzling conversation. The guests she must impress are 32-year-old entertainer David, 69-year-old Norwegian plain-talker Vivi, 47-year-old super-competitive Neil and 25-year-old media sales worker Charlotte. With risky menu choices and a fellow diner's peculiar laughter taking the spotlight, will the conversations ranging from lookalikes to riding polar bears be enough to secure Shanice the prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Retired estate agent Vivi hosts the second dinner party in and around Guildford. She is convinced her experience and original Scandinavian fayre can beat her less experienced competition and the soiree features Norwegian fish soup, ghost stories, ghost impressions, Norwegian singing and nursery rhymes

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sales manager Charlotte hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in and around Guildford, hoping to impress her guests enough to win the £1,000 prize money. However, she has taken a huge risk with an Asian-inspired menu, which could prove to be too exotic for entertainments manager David

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The penultimate day in and around Guildford, Surrey, sees children's entertainments manager David playing host. Having worked in the entertainments industry all his life, he is no stranger to showing people a good time, and employs his colleagues to put on a performance, while keeping his food simple. Is he forgetting the key ingredients of a dinner party?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final day in and around Guildford, Surrey, sales director Neil devises a sophisticated menu, dismissing his predecessors' efforts as unambitious. However, his unusual fayre is tempered by his choice of yodelling entertainment, which threatens to hinder his chance of scooping the £1,000 prize

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lee Juggurnauth jets off to the historic town of Mojácar in Spain's Almería region to help house-hunters Christine and Howard find the perfect holiday home. After 30 years of marriage and a few difficult years back at home, the couple have decided it's finally the right time to fulfil their long-held dream of buying a place in the sun. Lee sources five properties within their £50,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sports fan and rugby coach Matt loves all-things Italian and dreams of buying a holiday home near the historic city of Lucca in Tuscany. He's looking for a property with two bedrooms in a lively location, and brings along his best friend Shaun for some straight-talking advice. Jean Johansson secures the options of five properties for him to vivist within his £190,000 budget

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help the Clayton-Fishers, who have fallen out of love with their home in Walthamstow, east London. Sarah and her wife Pepper have opposing views on what to do with their two-bedroom terrace, as Sarah thinks it is time to move on, but Pepper is digging her heels in and wants to convert the loft

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: In the seaside town of Weymouth, Dorset, the presenter helps a couple whose first-time family home was destroyed overnight by a flash flood. When they bought their 19th-century coach house in 2018, they had planned to renovate its higgledy-piggledy layout, but the overnight disaster means they've got a far bigger project than they bargained for. The architect also visits Chiswick, west London, to assist a couple whose master bedroom and bathroom he redesigned a couple of years ago. With a growing family, next on their demolition list is the pokey kitchen

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jean Johansson heads to the glorious Greek island of Crete to help Jeanette and Phil, who are dreaming of starting a new, relaxed way of life in the sunshine while spending more quality time together. John recounts stories of royal connections - which fails to impress hotel managers Mark and Layla. In the morning, as they tuck in to a self-service breakfast, guest Tim decides that DIY doesn't work at Anstey Hall, but when he notes this on his feedback form, John and Johnny accuse him of game-playing

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the competition is to clothes-free Clover Spa and Hotel in Birmingham, where owner Tim Higgs hopes to convert his rivals to the joys of naturism. Judging with an extra critical eye are John and Jonny De Bruyne, who received negative feedback from Tim, and a thorough room inspection reveals a dusty porn DVD. Tim tries to encourage his nervous guests to take part in a naked spa experience, but John opts to watch television instead. When feedback is invited, Tim is delighted to discover Mark and Layla would stay again but shocked by the 'porn film' discovery

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the Angel Hotel in Coleford, Gloucestershire for the third day of the competition, where a treasure hunt organised by hosts Mark and Layla brings out their guests' competitive sides. Meanwhile, there is confusion at breakfast and Layla's manners are criticised

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at Higher Gitcombe in Totnes, Devon, where owners Paul and Lynnie Jolly set out to spoil their guests. Everyone is overwhelmed by the added extras, but naturist Tim is put off by cushions and clutter. After a masterclass in Nordic walking, Lynnie serves up a veg smoothie, which unhealthy eaters Mark and Layla dubiously down in one. Over dinner, Tim challenges his host's organic 'niche'. And Layla, still smarting from his negative feedback, labels him 'weird, naked Tim'. At breakfast, guests are wary as Tim's order goes wrong and Jonny and Layla engage in an eating competition. When feedback is invited, the Jollys are affronted by accusations of special treatment and vow to put the record straight

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On the final day of the competition, the owners of Anstey Hall, the Clover Spa and Hotel, the Angel Hotel and Higher Gitcombe meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and settle some scores. Having aired their issues, the winners are announced

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer search for properties in south and east London. Sisters Ruth and Anna are pooling funds, while Aba, also a first-time buyer, confesses to champagne tastes, but lemonade money

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts as five more players go head-to-head in the second heat of six rounds. Who'll get the highest score to secure a place in the Grand Final?

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 75-year-old man who fell out of bed is suspected of having a stroke, and doctors have only a short window of time to administer a life-saving, clot-busting drug. A mother of two falls off her horse, and a six-year-old girl has a possible neck injury after an accident in the playground. Family members of the patients share their personal stories

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Mark, 32, is brought to resus with a head injury after having an epileptic seizure, and he is sent for an urgent CT scan to assess the damage to his brain. Anastasia, 80, comes to urgent care with a pain in her leg and while she is sent for an X-ray to rule out a blood clot, she shares the story of her arrival to study in England from Nigeria, only for civil war to break out back home. Porter Chris, who's been part of the A&E department for 25 years, shares his insights into the job as he goes about his day

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics treat a woman who's fallen headfirst through a glass door, leaving a deep wound across her head, and a man complaining of crushing headaches who has a family history of brain haemorrhages. They also attend a car that's embedded in a garden wall after a two-vehicle smash and are called out to a drunk man who's been found collapsed in the street and is clutching his chest. The paramedics turn detectives to try to figure out why a woman keeps passing out

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The return of the documentary about life inside an A&E department, following patients treated in the same 24-hour period at St George's in south-west London. Fourteen-year-old Eduard is rushed in after falling from a swing and fracturing his ankle, while 62-year-old Rosina is treated having broken her wrist on the dancefloor at a family party. Paula is in Resus having been unable to take in food through her feeding tube and it is not only her health that is in danger, but also that of her unborn baby

The quick thinking by the crew led by Captain Peter Burkill prevented the incident from being fatal and saved all 152 people on board

programme.title: WWI: The Last Tommies
programme.desc: Veterans of the First World War recall their experiences, with archive footage used to illustrate events, beginning with the first two years of the war in 1914 and '15. The men - aged 90 and more when interviewed - reveal how they survived and kept their spirits up in the trenches with death all around them, while there is a look at how families at home faced up to the absence and loss of husbands, fathers, sweethearts and son

programme.title: WWI: The Last Tommies
programme.desc: An immersive account of one of the most tragic battles in history, the Battle of the Somme, told through its last survivors and through families and communities at home who lost loved ones

programme.title: WWI: The Last Tommies
programme.desc: In the concluding episode, veterans look back on the First World War battles of 1917 and 1918, and reflect on how Britain and her Allies turned a looming defeat into victory. The veterans recall dramatic stories of Passchendaele in 1917, the German army's Spring Offensive in 1918, and the British troop's final push to victory

programme.title: America's Great War 1917-1918
programme.desc: The presence of American soldiers - nicknamed the 'Sammies' - on French soil during the Great War is virtually unknown. Yet two million arrived in France between 1917 and 1918 to fight on the bloody battlefields alongside French and British troops

programme.title: Pandemic - The Flu That Killed 50 Million
programme.desc: Docu-drama about the 1918 Flu pandemic, which killed more than 50 million people. Using dramatic reconstruction and eyewitness testimony from doctors, soldiers, civilians and politicians, this one-off brings to life the onslaught of the disease and the horrors of those who lived through it. Narrated by Christopher Eccleston

programme.title: Forts of Verdun

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part one of two. The body of an elderly woman is found in a cave, with her fingerprints burnt off and a posy of foxgloves at her side that she did not pick herself. Jack is apprehensive about the arrival of new boss Professor Harriet Maven (Maggie Steed)

As the violence threatens to spiral into all-out war, will Helen and Elliot find the answers they've been looking for?

programme.title: Masters: Keepers of Tradition
programme.desc: The work of thatcher Marika Leen. She came from the Netherlands as a teenager, climbed onto a leaking roof in rural Galway on the instructions of three old bachelors below, and never really came down. Forty-five years later, she is one of Ireland's finest thatchers. In this edition, Marika returns to Inis Oírr to re-thatch the last thatched cottage on the island

programme.desc: The high stakes showdown between President Donald Trump and the US courts over presidential power. Featuring insight from Trump allies, opponents and experts on how he tests the extent of his power, the legal pushback and impact on the rule of law

programme.desc: Eleven-year-old Arian travels to Spain, discovering flamenco, cooking a family paella, and exploring her heritage

programme.title: news2day Weekly
programme.desc: The stories making the headlines both at home and abroad during the past seven days

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Remi apologises to Eden, Dana is over the moon at the positive news of Sonny's recovery, and Abigail is still unaware that Mali is using her garage as his workshop

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: David introduces Cash to the station's newest recruit - probationary constable Richard Brezniac. Roo presses Alf about booking his medical assessment

programme.title: Meet Me in New York (2022)
programme.desc: An ambitious events planner meets her match when a website editor hires her to organise his niece's birthday. Romantic drama, starring Brooke Nevin and Corey Sevier

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Preview
programme.desc: A look ahead to the tournament, which takes place in the USA, Mexico and Canada

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Preview
programme.desc: A look ahead to the tournament, which takes place in the USA, Mexico and Canada

programme.title: The Sunday Game Live
programme.desc: Monaghan v Mayo (Throw-in 4.00pm). Joanne Cantwell presents coverage of the All-Ireland Senior Football Championship first-round match at St Tiernach's Park. Monaghan qualified after finishing as runners-up to Armagh in the Ulster Senior Football Championship, and Mayo were semi-finalists in the Connacht Senior Football Championship. With analysis from Conor McManus and Lee Keegan

programme.title: The Spy Who Loved Me (1977)
programme.desc: James Bond teams up with a KGB agent to recover missing British and Soviet nuclear submarines, before whoever stole them can use their warheads. Unfortunately, 007 was responsible for the death of his new ally's lover - and she is determined to make him pay before their mission is complete. Spy adventure, starring Roger Moore, Barbara Bach, Curt Jurgens and Richard Kiel

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: Documentary showcasing the wonders, secrets and fragilities of the American continents, beginning with the creatures that live along North America's Atlantic coast, from the wild horses of Chesapeake Bay and the young raccoon learning the challenges of urban survival in New York City to the young black bears foraging in the Appalachian forests. Up in the air, bald eagles chase ospreys for a hard-won meal, while beneath the waters sand tiger sharks act as unlikely guardians for schools of scad fish. Narrated by Tom Hanks

programme.title: The Sunday Game
programme.desc: Des Cahill introduces highlights and discussion of first-round matches in the All-Ireland Senior Football Championship, including Dublin v Louth at Croke Park and Monaghan v Mayo at St Tiernach's Park

programme.title: Cloch Le Carn - Micheál
programme.desc: A portrait of GAA legend Micheal O Muircheartaigh, the voice of a nation, the king of commentators, who travelled the world promoting the game he loved. Featuring contributions from members of the O Muircheartaigh family, GAA legends Eddie Keher, Mick O'Connell, Cora Staunton, Kieran Donaghy and Eoin Liston, former president Mary McAleese and many more

programme.title: Shortscreen
programme.desc: A woman's very public break-up is saved when an Elvis impersonator comes to her rescue, and as the pair reminisce they discover meaning in their newly shared bond

programme.title: Blood Money: Inside the Soviet Economy
programme.desc: Two-part documentary exploring how the USSR - once seen as a second-rate industrial power, economically inferior to Germany, the USA and the UK - managed to defeat Hitler's armies and secure its place among the victors

programme.title: Dolphins: Wonders of the Ocean
programme.desc: Part one of two. Underwater cameraman Ken O'Sullivan brings viewers on his 20-year journey with dolphins, beginning with an encounter with a lone, lost dolphin in his home place in Clare. Footage captures these underwater mammals hunting and their everyday behaviours. There is also a look at how human activity is threatening these animals in Irish Atlantic waters

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: When Olivia hosts a surprise murder mystery party on the night of Frasier's second date with Holly, he is left with another major dilemma. However, a real-life disaster changes everything

programme.title: The Rise of RFK Jr
programme.desc: The dramatic and controversial rise of Robert F Kennedy Jr, from the tragedy and scandal of his personal life to the way he is reshaping government and public health

programme.title: Sport vs Money with Simon Jordan

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: The team investigate the killing of an Asian shopkeeper who tried to forbid dope dealers from operating outside his store. His killing leads detectives to Mahoney, whose parting shot to Kellerman throws the detective into a tailspin of depression

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Pembleton and Lewis are assigned to the murder of a wealthy woman. programme.title: Sort Of
programme.desc: A medical emergency forces Sabi to go to the ER, Wolf cracks when pressured to reveal secrets to Raffo and Aqsa has an emergency of her own

programme.title: The Hangover Part III (2013)
programme.desc: Drinking buddies Phil and Stu try to help their troubled friend Alan get his life in order, only to stumble into another bizarre misadventure. The Mob have taken Alan's brother hostage and to free him, they have to track down manic gangster Mr Chow, who has escaped from a Thai prison. Comedy sequel, starring Bradley Cooper, Ed Helms, Zach Galifianakis and John Goodman

programme.title: The Cinema List: Bingeable
programme.desc: On the Cinema List, join us as we explore our favourite franchises to get cosy with, from Transformers to Bad Boys 2. Snuggle down on the sofa and take your pick

programme.title: Just Go with It (2011)
programme.desc: A plastic surgeon pretends to be unhappily married to seduce women. When his girlfriend finds his prop wedding ring, he claims he is getting a divorce and has to persuade his assistant to pose as his soon-to-be ex-wife, but is forced to come up with yet more lies to maintain the illusion. Romantic comedy, starring Adam Sandler, Jennifer Aniston and Nicole Kidman

programme.title: The Karate Kid (2010)
programme.desc: An American youngster goes to live in China with his mother, where he becomes an outsider and falls foul of the local bullies. He receives help from the kindly school caretaker, who turns out to be a kung fu master and offers to train the boy to defend himself. Martial arts drama, with Jaden Smith and Jackie Chan

programme.title: Little Fockers (2010)
programme.desc: Nurse Greg is now the proud father of twins, but has yet to earn the respect of his wife's stern father after 10 years of marriage. The couple invite both sides of their extended family to the kids' birthday party, where Greg makes a desperate effort to finally prove his worth to his father-in-law. Comedy sequel, with Ben Stiller, Robert De Niro, Owen Wilson and Dustin Hoffman

programme.title: Instant Family (2018)
programme.desc: After being taunted by relatives who think they will never have kids, married couple Pete and Ellie Wagner consider the option of adopting a child. However, they get more than they bargained for when they take in a rebellious teen and her two siblings. Director Sean Anders' comedy, starring Mark Wahlberg, Rose Byrne and Isabela Merced

programme.title: Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle (2017)
programme.desc: Four teenagers play a jungle-themed game on an old console, only to find themselves transported into the game and transformed into its heroic characters. They must embark on a death-defying journey through the game's wilderness setting to make it back to the real world, or be stuck as video game characters forever. Fantasy action comedy, starring Dwayne Johnson, Karen Gillan, Kevin Hart and Jack Black

programme.title: Jumanji: The Next Level (2019)
programme.desc: Players of Jumanji return, but as they attempt to rescue one of their own, they will have to brave parts unknown from arid deserts to snowy mountains, to escape the world's most dangerous game. Director Jake Kasdan's fantasy adventure sequel to his 2017 rebooted hit Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle, starring Dwayne Johnson, Karen Gillan, Kevin Hart and Jack Black

programme.title: Anchorman: The Legend of Ron Burgundy (2004)
programme.desc: The chauvinistic host of a top-rated American news programme in 1970s San Diego fights for his status and job security when both are threatened by the arrival of an ambitious female reporter, whose determination to become a newsreader leads to a bitter battle of the sexes. Comedy, starring Will Ferrell, Christina Applegate, Fred Willard and Steve Carell

programme.title: The Hangover Part II (2011)
programme.desc: Phil, Stu and Alan reunite in Bangkok for another stag weekend, but having learned the lessons of their previous misadventures, resolve to keep this one quiet and alcohol-free. Things inevitably do not go according to plan, and they must once again piece together the events of a wild night they cannot remember. Comedy sequel, starring Bradley Cooper, Ed Helms and Zach Galifianakis

programme.title: The Hangover Part III (2013)
programme.desc: Drinking buddies Phil and Stu try to help their troubled friend Alan get his life in order, only to stumble into another bizarre misadventure. The Mob have taken Alan's brother hostage and to free him, they have to track down manic gangster Mr Chow, who has escaped from a Thai prison. Comedy sequel, starring Bradley Cooper, Ed Helms, Zach Galifianakis and John Goodman

programme.title: Anchorman: The Legend of Ron Burgundy (2004)
programme.desc: The chauvinistic host of a top-rated American news programme in 1970s San Diego fights for his status and job security when both are threatened by the arrival of an ambitious female reporter, whose determination to become a newsreader leads to a bitter battle of the sexes. Comedy, starring Will Ferrell, Christina Applegate, Fred Willard and Steve Carell

programme.title: Instant Family (2018)
programme.desc: After being taunted by relatives who think they will never have kids, married couple Pete and Ellie Wagner consider the option of adopting a child. However, they get more than they bargained for when they take in a rebellious teen and her two siblings. Director Sean Anders' comedy, starring Mark Wahlberg, Rose Byrne and Isabela Merced

programme.title: Sex Tape (2014)
programme.desc: A married couple decide to put some passion back into their dull marriage by filming themselves having sex. However, a technological mix-up results in the footage being uploaded to various gadgets, and they race against time to recover all the copies before it ends up in the public eye. Comedy, starring Cameron Diaz and Jason Segel

programme.title: Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again (2018)
programme.desc: It's five years since Sophie invited her three possible dads to her wedding, and now most of her family are reunited as she prepares for the grand reopening of the Hotel Bella Donna. Along the way, she learns more about her mother's past. Abba-themed musical sequel, starring Amanda Seyfried, Lily James, Pierce Brosnan, Cher and Andy Garcia

programme.title: American Reunion (2012)
programme.desc: Jim Levenstein and his old friends return to their home town for a high-school reunion. Struck by how much their lives have changed, they set out to relive the sexual misadventures of their adolescence, but only end up making bigger fools of themselves than they did as teenagers. Comedy sequel to the American Pie movies, starring Jason Biggs and Alyson Hannigan

programme.title: Look Who's Talking Too (1990)
programme.desc: Wisecracking toddler Mikey is joined by mischievous baby sister Julie and sibling rivalry inevitably erupts - while their parents have problems of their own to deal with, in the shape of the kids' good-for-nothing uncle. Comedy sequel, starring John Travolta and Kirstie Alley, with the voices of Bruce Willis and Rosanne Barr

programme.title: Blazing Saddles (1974)
programme.desc: A crooked railway tycoon whose men are terrorising a frontier town arranges for a black sheriff to be appointed, hoping the racist residents will reject him. The lawman proves more cunning than expected, and with the help of a drunken gunslinger, runs rings around the businessman. Mel Brooks' Western spoof, in which he co-stars alongside Cleavon Little, Gene Wilder and Madeline Kahn

programme.title: Ferris Bueller's Day Off (1986)
programme.desc: A teenager plays truant from school and spends the day dragging his girlfriend and a reluctant friend into all manner of crazy adventures in the big city - while the school's dean of students vows to stop at nothing to prove the youngster is faking illness. Comedy, starring Matthew Broderick, Mia Sara, Alan Ruck, Jennifer Grey, Jeffrey Jones and Charlie Sheen

programme.title: Multiplicity (1996)
programme.desc: A workaholic whose demanding job often keeps him away from home hits on the idea of cloning himself so he can spend more time with his wife and kids. However, this is when his problems really begin, as the clones start to take over his house. Comedy, starring Michael Keaton, Andie MacDowell, Harris Yulin and Richard Masur

programme.title: The Blues Brothers (1980)
programme.desc: Two criminal brothers discover the orphanage where they were raised faces closure unless the nuns who run it can pay their tax bill, so the pair embark on an urgent mission to revive their old blues band to raise the money needed. Comedy, starring John Belushi, Dan Aykroyd, Aretha Franklin, James Brown, Ray Charles, Cab Calloway, Carrie Fisher, John Candy and John Lee Hooker

programme.title: Trading Places (1983)
programme.desc: Two billionaire brothers engineer the downfall of a high-flying stockbroker, while giving a homeless conman all the advantages of wealth, as part of a bet to see whether heredity or upbringing determines character. Comedy, starring Dan Aykroyd, Eddie Murphy, Jamie Lee Curtis, Ralph Bellamy, Don Ameche and Denholm Elliott

programme.title: Caddyshack (1980)
programme.desc: A host of weird and wacky characters cause mayhem at an exclusive golf club, including a laid-back professional player, an obnoxious millionaire, the snobbish club president, a motley collection of teenage caddies and a psychotic groundskeeper engaging in a fight to the death with a canny gopher. Comedy, starring Chevy Chase, Bill Murray, Michael O'Keefe and Rodney Dangerfield

programme.title: Ferris Bueller's Day Off (1986)
programme.desc: A teenager plays truant from school and spends the day dragging his girlfriend and a reluctant friend into all manner of crazy adventures in the big city - while the school's dean of students vows to stop at nothing to prove the youngster is faking illness. Comedy, starring Matthew Broderick, Mia Sara, Alan Ruck, Jennifer Grey, Jeffrey Jones and Charlie Sheen

programme.title: The Breakfast Club (1985)
programme.desc: Five teenagers who occupy different areas of their school's social structure are thrown together for a Saturday morning detention. During the day, they analyse their behaviour and find unexpected common ground. Drama, directed by John Hughes and starring Emilio Estevez, Judd Nelson, Molly Ringwald, Anthony Michael Hall, Ally Sheedy and Paul Gleason

programme.title: Trading Places (1983)
programme.desc: Two billionaire brothers engineer the downfall of a high-flying stockbroker, while giving a homeless conman all the advantages of wealth, as part of a bet to see whether heredity or upbringing determines character. programme.title: Fuze (2026)
programme.desc: An unexploded Second World War bomb is discovered on a busy construction site in the centre of London. Chaos ensues as the military and police begin a mass evacuation. Drama, starring Aaron Taylor-Johnson, Theo James, Gugu Mbatha-Raw and Sam Worthington

programme.title: Him
programme.desc: Cameron Cade is predicted to be the next great quarterback of American football. During a night-time training session, he suffers a traumatic brain injury. Cameron agrees to attend a week-long boot camp run by retiring star quarterback Isaiah White at his secluded desert compound. However, Cade's life soon turns into a nightmare. Supernatural horror, starring Marlon Wayans and Tyriq Withers

programme.title: The Cinema List: Race Against Time
programme.desc: This edition is about the race against time. From a deadly threat in Fuze to The Desperate Hour, where Naomi Watts faces every parent's nightmare - the clock is ticking

programme.title: Tafiti - Across The Desert (2025)
programme.desc: A young meerkat's grandpa is bitten by a snake, so he sets off to find a magical flower for the cure. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Cosima Henman, Steve Hudson and Kathleen Renish

programme.title: The Plague (2025)
programme.desc: A socially awkward tween endures the ruthless hierarchy at a water polo camp as his anxiety spirals into psychological turmoil over the summer. Thriller, starring Joel Edgerton and Everett Blunck

programme.desc: When Gabby's prized dollhouse ends up with eccentric cat lady Vera, the youngster embarks on a adventure to reunite the Gabby Cats and retrieve her beloved diorama before it's too late. Comedy adventure, starring Laila Lockhart Kraner and Kristen Wiig

programme.title: Downton Abbey: The Grand Finale (2025)

programme.title: Tina (2021)
programme.desc: Born Anna Mae Bullock in 1939, Tina Turner has sold in excess of 100 million records and earned numerous plaudits including Grammy awards and induction into the Rock And Roll Hall Of Fame with her first husband, Ike Turner. She was the first woman to grace the cover of Rolling Stone magazine and dabbled in acting including a memorable performance opposite Mel Gibson in Mad Max Beyond Thunderdome. Following a prolonged period of ill health, Turner intends to retire from the spotlight with her second husband, Erwin Bach. She bids farewell to her fans in the emotional documentary Tina. Directed by Dan Lindsay and TJ Martin, the film invites the singer to reminisce about her turbulent life and career with glowing contributions from friends, family and fans including Oprah Winfrey and Angela Bassett

programme.desc: Raksha Dave and Alex Langlands search for the remains of a First World War stable at Catterick Garrison in North Yorkshire, where thousands of horses would have been housed and trained for the front line. programme.title: Invasion: The Normans
programme.desc: Two-part documentary exploring the Anglo-Norman invasion of Ireland. The first edition opens in the turmoil of 12th-century Ireland, where provincial kings compete for supremacy. programme.title: Invasion: The Normans
programme.desc: The second of the two-part documentary follows the course of the invasion, as Strongbow wins victory after victory. In 1171 Henry II arrived to takes control of Ireland, and he granted land there to settlers from Britain. Now archaeology and cutting-edge science reveal their fascinating lives. The episode also shows how the conquest of Ireland enabled centuries of war between England and France

programme.title: Ancient Superstructures
programme.desc: The Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem is regarded by many as the most sacred Christian site in the world. Every year, the site welcomes more than one million pilgrims. Built on the site of the death and resurrection of Jesus of Nazareth, it has a unique atmosphere. Defied by time, wars and earthquakes, the Holy Sepulchre was rebuilt many times, and has transformed itself into an architectural labyrinth. Here, thanks to the use of new technologies, the Holy Sepulchre reveals its secrets as never before

programme.title: Ancient Superstructures

programme.desc: The actor is joined by astrophysicist Sarah Cruddas to recall the events of October 2017, when astronomers at the Pan-STARRS observatory saw an object they named Oumuamua, the very first observed visitor from interstellar space. programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts spends a year visiting archaeological digs around the country, finding out about discoveries that shed new light on British history. In Buckinghamshire, Simon Mays and Jill Eyers use a recent rediscovery of artefacts from a 1912 excavation to investigate what appears to have been a mass infanticide during the Roman era. Alice also meets the team examining one of the largest discoveries of Roman coins ever made in Britain, and hears about a mystery in Dorset

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits Happisburgh in Norfolk, where the discovery of rare artefacts provides evidence of the earliest human occupation in Britain, challenging the conventional belief that mankind would not have been able to survive in northern Europe one million years ago. She also joins a team at Forteviot in Scotland, where the uncovering of a Bronze Age grave holds a big surprise

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts travels to Bamburgh, Northumberland, to learn about the generations of Anglo Saxons who lived and worked there, gaining an insight into the illnesses they suffered and the societies in which they lived. She visits the Sutton Hoo site, as well as a pioneering project in Sittingbourne, Kent, which was set up following the unearthing of an Anglo Saxon cemetery, and looks at the Staffordshire hoard discovered by a metal detector in 2009

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts finds out about discoveries that shed light on the Tudor Age, visiting excavations at Shakespeare's first theatre in London's Shoreditch and at his last home in Stratford-upon-Avon, where clues reveal his economical use of money. In Wales, she meets a team of archaeologists learning about the realities of Henry VIII's dissolution of the monasteries, while the history of a forgotten royal palace is uncovered on the banks of the Thames

programme.title: Digging for Britain

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts spends a year visiting archaeological digs around the country, finding out about discoveries that shed new light on British history. programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores archaeological digs at sites dating back to the Viking age, offering an insight into how the seafaring warriors from Scandinavia lived across Britain. In the Outer Hebrides, she examines signs of the earliest Norse settlers, before inspecting a collection of silver and gold pieces discovered in York. There is also a look at some of the most treasured Viking finds, including 12th-century ivory chess figures the Lewis Chessmen

programme.title: Digging for Britain

programme.title: Big Cats: An Amazing Animal Family
programme.desc: Part one of two. Biologist Patrick Aryee explores how cats have evolved throughout the millennia, learning to thrive in inhospitable environments, as well as becoming the world's most popular pet. In the first edition there is spectacular footage of caracals jumping three metres to catch flying birds, and a rare Bornean bay cat, which was thought to have been extinct. Patrick finds out how tigers made the transition from South-east Asia's steamy jungles to the -40C temperatures of Siberia, and meets 'lion whisperer' Kevin Richardson

programme.title: Big Cats: An Amazing Animal Family
programme.desc: Patrick Aryee concludes his two-part exploration of the feline family tree, revealing how the Canadian lynx learned to thrive in the snow and spread out, evolving and inhabiting new parts of the Americas. In Belize, he mounts an experiment that showcases the margay's supersharp senses and gravity-defying acrobatics, and heads to the training track with a cheetah to find out what speed it is capable of. Finally, Patrick tells the story of the cat's domestication in Essaouira, Morocco, believed to be one of the first places where one particular species first gave up life in the wild in return for food, warmth, security and a home

programme.title: The Bachelor King
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the lives of king penguins, filmed on the sub-Antarctic island of South Georgia over five months. The programme imagines what one penguin's life would be, following it during the challenging, awkward time between adolescence and adulthood. Presented by David Attenborough

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: How a pair of fake registration plates on a getaway car helped a detective to solve a series of robberies. Plus, footage of a supermarket cashier standing up to armed robbers

programme.title: Caught Red Handed

programme.title: Highway Patrol

programme.title: Land of the Far North

programme.title: In for the Kill
programme.desc: Footage of one of the most dramatic moments in nature - a predator claiming its prey, revealing how these creatures have adapted to achieve epic feats of speed, cunning and agility

programme.title: The Ocean's Greatest Feast
programme.desc: The sardine run along the east coast of South Africa, a challenging voyage for the fish as they navigate the ocean, barely escaping the hungry predators who depend on them for food

programme.title: Ocean Parks
programme.desc: A visit to Monterey Bay to look at the US's largest marine sanctuary - home to 525 species of fish and a huge, underwater kelp forest

programme.title: Ocean Parks
programme.desc: The Mesoamerican Reef in the Caribbean Sea, which has been jeopardised by overfishing and tourism, putting its sharks and turtles in grave danger

programme.title: Cub Camp
programme.desc: Cameras follow the summer cubs as they learn to climb trees and forage for food in preparation for their release into the wild

programme.title: Cub Camp
programme.desc: Cameras follow the summer cubs as they get ready for release with Roger, before meeting Ace and Little Blueberry, two sick newbies staying with the Z cubs in winter

programme.title: City Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: The vets wrestle an uncooperative turkey vulture onto the X-ray table to reveal a shoulder injury. A baby groundhog is rushed in after a car accident

programme.title: City Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: A mother rabbit cares for an adopted baby, as the staff assesses the little bunny's safety. Can this odd couple stay together?

programme.title: City Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: The interesting cases of two red-tailed hawks, who need help getting back into fighting shape. Plus, the medical team brings in a feisty, rare white squirrel

programme.title: City Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: The centre's resident turkey vulture is ready for release and in search of a flock. The medical team is baffled by a toad who is mysteriously taken ill

programme.title: Into the Wild: India
programme.desc: Alpha male monkey Rajan sits at the top of an unusually large troop. He has done well to head up such a big tribe. But the future is not looking so good

programme.title: Into the Wild: India
programme.desc: A gang of young males primates attack, forcing the monkey troop of Jodhpur's Temple Gardens to flee, through unfamiliar terrain. First they need to find food. Then they can pursue revenge

programme.title: Woodpeckers: The Hole Story
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the unique abilities of the woodpecker to reveal what makes this bird so special. Narrated by Paul Giamatti

programme.title: Deep Dive North America
programme.desc: Wildlife biologist Lizzie Daly explores North America's coastlines, encountering flourishing wildlife and meeting the people risking it all to preserve and protect these underwater locations and their inhabitants

programme.title: Deep Dive North America
programme.desc: Lizzie Daly heads north to tag sharks and witness first-hand the slow, sobering disappearance of sea ice

programme.title: Predators
programme.desc: In the remote mountains of Patagonia, a female puma has just six months to teach its cubs how to survive in an increasingly competitive world

programme.title: Predators
programme.desc: In Zimbabwe, an African wild dog pack find themselves outnumbered by lions and hyenas, and the pups must learn quickly if the pack is to survive

programme.title: Aussie Snake Wranglers: Deadly Pursuit
programme.desc: Jess and Dan grab a smelly snake before Stu rescues one at university

programme.title: Aussie Snake Wranglers: Deadly Pursuit

programme.title: David Attenborough's First Life
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part documentary, the naturalist explores the origins of life on Earth. Beginning the journey near his childhood home in Charnwood Forest, Leicestershire, he learns how a fossil discovery transformed scientists' understanding of the evolution of complex organisms, and in Newfoundland and Australia he reveals the earliest kinds of animal to exist on the planet. With the help of cutting-edge technology and visual effects, the programme brings the creatures to life, demonstrating how they survived

programme.title: David Attenborough's First Life
programme.desc: In the conclusion of the two-part documentary, the naturalist travels to Burgess Shale in Canada's Rocky Mountains, where he explores how evolution gained pace once creatures developed mouths and mobility. In Scotland, he reveals that one of the smallest fossils found holds the secret to how animals began surviving on dry land, and explains the bloom of life during the oxygen-rich Carboniferous period

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural History Museum Alive
programme.desc: The broadcaster invites viewers to join him as he takes an after-hours stroll around one of his favourite places, the Natural History Museum. But this is no ordinary tour as some of the many exhibits spring to life as the veteran naturalist strolls along

programme.title: Aussie Snake Wranglers: Deadly Pursuit

programme.title: Land of the Far North
programme.desc: Life in the Arctic Circle, where the sun hardly sets in summer. Food is abundant, but animals and plants alike face great challenges such as sleep deprivation, pests and predators

programme.title: Dogs with Extraordinary Jobs
programme.desc: Documentary about dogs that help humans in surprising ways, including a Great Dane that has helped a 13-year-old girl with a muscle-wasting condition to walk, swim and even ride a bike. Plus, the stories of a Border collie mix who finds survivors during earthquakes, a labrador-German Shepherd mix who helps stop ivory poaching, and a team of water rescue dogs in Italy

programme.title: Rewilding
programme.desc: Documentary about the European bison, the continent's biggest terrestrial mammal, which was hunted to extinction but has been re-introduced thanks to rewilding

programme.title: Rewilding
programme.desc: A visit to Steinhuder Meer, a biodiversity hotspot in Germany, a combination of fenlands, alder march and dunes which create an amazing landscape

programme.title: Rewilding
programme.desc: Andalucia consists of extreme habitats once severely impacted by humans. Now, many previously near-extinct animals are reclaiming this unique land

programme.title: The Wild Wild East
programme.desc: The wildlife of the Maritime Provinces on Canada's eastern shore. The first edition visits Prince Edward Island to explore the family life of the red fox, sly creatures adept at living just on the fringes of society

programme.title: The Wild Wild East
programme.desc: Cameras take to the sky with a bald eagle, seeing how they raise families, hunt and forage for food, and compete for territory with another bird of prey, the osprey

programme.title: The Wild Wild East
programme.desc: The creatures following the ebb and flow of the tides, including sandpipers migrating from the Arctic to South America and seals making a home on Nova Scotia's rocky coastline

programme.desc: Land meets sea in The Garden Route, a famous park and wildlife paradise that's home to the humpback whale, the blue duiker antelope and the endangered Knysna seahorse

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Part one of two. Yvette Fielding begin a 2007 investigation at the Transylvanian home of the notorious Vlad 'The Impaler' Tepes, the man on whom the legend of Dracula was based. Originally broadcast live from Corvin's Castle, the boyhood home of Vlad Tepes, the first night of the ghost-hunt sees the team searching the Transylvanian Room, Desert Tower and the Hanging Gallery

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Part two of two. Yvette Fielding and the team continue their 2007 Transylvanian investigation at the home of the notorious Vlad 'The Impaler' Tepes, the man on whom the legend of Dracula was based. The second night in Corvin's Castle sees them search the Guards Passageway, the Old Gate Tower, the White Tower and the Bethlen Wing.

programme.title: Batman Returns (1992)
programme.desc: The masked hero returns to face the Penguin, a grotesque crime boss dwelling in the sewers who conspires with a corrupt businessman to make himself mayor. When the tycoon tries to kill his secretary, she returns as vengeful vigilante Catwoman, who soon forms an intense relationship with the dark knight in both their costumed and real identities. Tim Burton's comic-book adventure sequel, starring Michael Keaton, Danny DeVito and Michelle Pfeiffer

programme.title: Batman Forever (1995)
programme.desc: The masked crimefighter takes on the twin threats of disfigured maniac Two-Face and insane genius the Riddler, who are plotting to control the brains of the entire world. Meanwhile, an acrobat whose family has been murdered by Two-Face steps up to join Batman in his fight against evil. Superhero adventure sequel, starring Val Kilmer, Tommy Lee Jones, Nicole Kidman, Jim Carrey and Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: Batman & Robin (1997)
programme.desc: The caped crusader and his sidekick take on the villainous Mr Freeze, who plans to destroy Gotham City with an icy doomsday weapon unless his demands are met. The dynamic duo also have to contend with femme fatale Poison Ivy, who is determined to drive a wedge between them. Comic-book adventure, starring George Clooney, Arnold Schwarzenegger, Uma Thurman and Chris O'Donnell

programme.desc: Animated comedy series. To raise awareness of local squirrels' cavity problems, Sam wants to gather as many people as possible for a demonstration, a 'perch-in' in the trees in front of the town hall. She asks the Sceptre for the power to be super-persuasive, but the power works in reverse and Sam is only persuasive when she turns her back - when she faces people, no-one pays any attention to her! She and Mo end up having to work together, all while having a flock of followers at their backs.

programme.title: 50/50 Heroes
programme.desc: Animated comedy series. Amber organises the ExtravaDance, a contest where each student has to perform his or her own dance on stage in front of the whole school. Sam goes into total panic - ever since she made a complete fool of herself by dancing like a jerk at the last school party, she's promised herself that she will never dance in front of anybody again. Sam asks the Sceptre to grant her the power to be a great dancer, only to end up dancing uncontrollably the moment she hears any music...

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: In the fourth episode, Julie Fowlis and Muireann Nic Amhlaoibh visit the Celtic heartlands of mainland Europe, accompanied by acclaimed musical friends from Scotland and Ireland. Starting in Brittany, they continue on to Asturias and Galicia in Northern Spain, regions with a strong Celtic heritage. In Brittany, they are joined by Scottish singer Mischa MacPherson and from Ireland, Laoise Kelly. They explore Galicia and Asturias and take in the music and culture of both regions with multi-instrumentalists Angus MacKenzie from Skye and Barry Kerr from Dublin

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: A Sheffield couple call upon Charlie Dimmock and Flo Headlam to help them with their bland, fake grass-clad back garden. Karen and Kathryn love to spend time outdoors - walking, reading, sunbathing, eating, playing with their dog - but desire a space less reminiscent of a football pitch. They have their hearts set on a garden with a forest feel, with informal, wandering paths and a log cabin vibe. They'd like trees to make them feel more enclosed, a barbecue zone and a quiet, restful spot for reading. They have a healthy budget of £6,500 but even so, Flo and Charlie have their work cut out

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: South African-born Linda, her husband Mark and Coco the cat share a one-bedroom floating home on Tagg's Island in Hampton, Surrey. It's an idyllic location, but their home is also their workplace and it's starting to feel a bit cramped. The couple want to stay put in their exclusive location, so Linda has hatched an ambitious plan to buy the boat next door and do it up all by herself. It's a huge challenge and it remains to be seen whether enthusiasm and passion will be enough to get the job done
