programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh visits a country garden in Berkshire and demonstrates how to replicate features including climbing clematis, rustic flower beds, ironwork benches and mosaic pathways. Food expert Valentine Warner sets about creating flavours from a rural plot, and Charlotte Uhlenbroek finds out what gardeners should do when hedgehogs appear

programme.title: Queer Lives Today
programme.desc: Anna Mouzouri's touching documentary following the aquatic escapades of two queer mermaids as they find belonging through mermaid races and mermaid conventions

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: A Sheffield couple call upon Charlie Dimmock and Flo Headlam to help them with their bland, fake grass-clad back garden. Karen and Kathryn love to spend time outdoors - walking, reading, sunbathing, eating, playing with their dog - but desire a space less reminiscent of a football pitch. They have their hearts set on a garden with a forest feel, with informal, wandering paths and a log cabin vibe. They'd like trees to make them feel more enclosed, a barbecue zone and a quiet, restful spot for reading. They have a healthy budget of £6,500 but even so, Flo and Charlie have their work cut out

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill travel to Trafford, Greater Manchester, to meet Hannah and Christie. The couple feel their boxy 42-square-metre garden doesn't reflect their fun-loving lifestyle. Chris is a DJ and Hannah plays the sax, and they believe the feel of a music festival would be a great theme for the team to incorporate into their plot

programme.title: Queer Lives Today
programme.desc: Shiva Raichandani's film follows Asifa as she prepares to undergo gender affirmation surgery, and the trans activist reflects on how her life has changed during her transition

programme.title: Girls Come First (1975)
programme.desc: Portrait artist Alan Street is employed by the Soho Swingers Club to paint the hostesses. Whilst he relishes his new job his girlfriend is less than impressed. Contains nudity.

programme.title: Come Play With Me (1977)
programme.desc: A nurse at a health farm takes over running the establishment, and comes up with her own range of sexual treatments. Meanwhile, two forgers on the run hide out at the farm. Sex comedy, starring Mary Millington, Alfie Bass and Irene Handl

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Horticulturist Alan Titchmarsh and a team of experts search for Britain's most attractive outdoor spaces in people's homes, and show how to re-create their looks. In the first edition, the broadcaster demonstrates how to achieve the classic suburban garden, complete with summer house, display beds and an area to produce honey, visiting a female gardener whose plot has been a labour of love

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh visits a country garden in Berkshire and demonstrates how to replicate features including climbing clematis, rustic flower beds, ironwork benches and mosaic pathways. Food expert Valentine Warner sets about creating flavours from a rural plot, and Charlotte Uhlenbroek finds out what gardeners should do when hedgehogs appear

programme.title: Country House Auction
programme.desc: Documentary following antiques as they are restored for sale at Castle Durrow. Expert jeweller Frances appraises a beautiful five-diamond platinum sparkler, and a Fender guitar signed by the Rolling Stones piques the interest of a dealer

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: An antiques expert, a market trader and an upcycler try to make the most money for BBC Children in Need, beginning with Melissa Downhill, Kevin Paxton and Arusha Irvine

programme.title: Fatal Fall: Murder or Accident?

programme.title: Irish Crime Files: Murder in the Mountains
programme.desc: The story of Phyllis Murphy, who vanished near Kildare in 1979. Her body wasn't found for a month, and the case went cold until new forensics emerged decades later.

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: Ellen Mallam is left to drown in an upturned car by its driver, but the young woman's connection to James Blackstone, an aristocratic family's heir apparent, impedes Gently and Bacchus's investigation. The chief inspector discovers that the son's future has been carefully mapped out since birth by his implacable mother Alethea, and she will not countenance any interference in her plans. However, an astonishing twist causes the detectives to reconsider the case in its entirety. Sixties-set detective drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby, with Geraldine Somerville, James Norton, Roger Lloyd Pack, Christopher Fairbank and Ebony Buckle

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A former music hall comedian swears revenge on the priest, leaving Father Brown trapped in a derelict building. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Flambeau enlists Fr Brown's help to steal the Queen's coronation gift from the Pope. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Lady Felicia's reckless driving lands her in the next hospital bed to Mrs McCarthy - with a killer on the loose. Mystery, starring Nancy Carroll and Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest investigates an apparent suicide, believing the death of a recently returned RAF pilot to be murder. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway: Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu investigate a case of paranormal activity when they are approached by a wealthy member of the local trainspotting community. Drama, starring Mark Benton and Jo Joyner

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway: Private Investigators
programme.desc: An anonymous message invites Frank to join a two-day Shakespearian walk, advising him a crime will be committed. Kitted up, Frank joins the other revellers. However, the idyllic event soon turns to disaster when one of the walkers is murdered and Frank becomes the prime suspect. Lu and Sebastian set about cracking the case in a bid to prove Frank's innocence. Mark Benton, Jo Joyner and Patrick Walshe McBride star

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: DI Jack Mooney has a fresh mystery to solve when zoo owner Xander Shepherd is killed by a tranquilliser dart in the middle of the grounds. With the park shut to the public at the time, suspicion falls on the victim's colleagues - but given they were all in the staff area when the shot was fired, how could any of them have done the deed? Meanwhile, Dwayne's replacement arrives in the shape of new officer Ruby Patterson - who also happens to be the Commissioner's niece - and Jack faces a mammoth task when he is signed up to the island's charity swim race

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The investigation into a brutal murder in a public toilet has Murdoch butting heads with Inspector Choi as he closes in on the killer

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: Rex sniffs out the secrets behind a groundbreaking gin recipe and the heirs to a distillery after the patriarch is drowned in his own spirits

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The presenter of TV holiday series Hot Shots takes a midnight swim, only for her body to be washed up on the shore. Initial evidence points towards accidental drowning, but Jack is troubled by marks on the victim's neck - and as he questions her colleagues, a secret affair, unrequited love and rocky working relationships convince the DI that this was no accident. With Florence away on a romantic weekend, JP steps up to help the DI with the case, and with the tourist board up in arms about the crime, Commissioner Selwyn keeps a close eye on the investigation

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest finds himself in a seemingly impossible situation when he is told during a confession that Bishop Talbot will be assassinated tomorrow. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.sub-title: The Secret of the Flame Tree
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Literary Festival is rocked by the death of a young literature student - and Florence's childhood friend - whose body is discovered at the bottom of a cliff. Suicide seems the likely cause, but as ever Humphrey isn't so keen to jump to conclusions - even with the presence of a note in the victim's bag. But with everyone the dead girl knew at the festival, who could have pushed her over the edge? Away from the investigation, the inspector is busy preparing the shack for Martha's stay. Sun-soaked whodunit, guest starring Fiona Allen, Ramon Tikaram and Monica Dolan

programme.title: The Mentalist
programme.desc: Lisbon and Rigsby travel to Crane Creek, where they examine the murder of a film-maker who has been shot at close range. The local police identify a man who was accused of killing his wife two years previously as the main suspect, but when the agents close in, he takes the employees and customers at City Hall hostage, including Jane, who was settling a parking fine at the time. Crime drama, starring Robin Tunney and Simon Baker

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: Charlie and Rex witness an armed robbery during a trip to the bank, and soon discover a connection to a string of ingenious unsolved heists

programme.title: Unforgettable
programme.desc: Carrie finds herself torn between her professional and personal life when her boyfriend Steve's close friend is suspected of a double homicide. When her relationship begins to suffer she presses herself to discover the evidence needed to clear the man's name, but in an act desperation she breaks protocol and warns her partner and his acquaintances of a potential arrest

programme.title: Return to Paradise
programme.desc: Metropolitan Police officer Mackenzie Clarke is deployed to her Australian home town of Dolphin Cove after being accused of falsifying evidence. Once there, she faces scorn from her old acquaintances due to her earlier abandoning her fiancé at the altar. When a real estate salesman is found stabbed to death on a beach during a surfing competition, Mackenzie has to swallow her pride and work with her jilted former love, a forensic pathologist, and her other old colleagues to solve this perplexing crime. Death in Paradise spin-off, starring Anna Samson and Lloyd Griffith

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest is stunned when he realises that the fugitive seeking sanctuary at St Mary's is Inspector Sullivan - and the man is wanted for murder. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams, Tom Chambers and Sorcha Cusack

programme.desc: Carrie finds herself torn between her professional and personal life when her boyfriend Steve's close friend is suspected of a double homicide. When her relationship begins to suffer she presses herself to discover the evidence needed to clear the man's name, but in an act desperation she breaks protocol and warns her partner and his acquaintances of a potential arrest

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.sub-title: The Secret of the Flame Tree
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Literary Festival is rocked by the death of a young literature student - and Florence's childhood friend - whose body is discovered at the bottom of a cliff. Suicide seems the likely cause, but as ever Humphrey isn't so keen to jump to conclusions - even with the presence of a note in the victim's bag. But with everyone the dead girl knew at the festival, who could have pushed her over the edge? Away from the investigation, the inspector is busy preparing the shack for Martha's stay. Sun-soaked whodunit, guest starring Fiona Allen, Ramon Tikaram and Monica Dolan

programme.title: Return to Paradise
programme.desc: Metropolitan Police officer Mackenzie Clarke is deployed to her Australian home town of Dolphin Cove after being accused of falsifying evidence. Once there, she faces scorn from her old acquaintances due to her earlier abandoning her fiancé at the altar. When a real estate salesman is found stabbed to death on a beach during a surfing competition, Mackenzie has to swallow her pride and work with her jilted former love, a forensic pathologist, and her other old colleagues to solve this perplexing crime. Death in Paradise spin-off, starring Anna Samson and Lloyd Griffith

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest is stunned when he realises that the fugitive seeking sanctuary at St Mary's is Inspector Sullivan - and the man is wanted for murder. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams, Tom Chambers and Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.sub-title: The Secret of the Flame Tree
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Literary Festival is rocked by the death of a young literature student - and Florence's childhood friend - whose body is discovered at the bottom of a cliff. Suicide seems the likely cause, but as ever Humphrey isn't so keen to jump to conclusions - even with the presence of a note in the victim's bag. But with everyone the dead girl knew at the festival, who could have pushed her over the edge? Away from the investigation, the inspector is busy preparing the shack for Martha's stay. Sun-soaked whodunit, guest starring Fiona Allen, Ramon Tikaram and Monica Dolan

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Humphrey and Martha's romantic weekend on a remote island is cut short when the hotel owner is stabbed to death in his room in what appears to be an attempted robbery. But since the flower beds outside the window are undisturbed, Humphrey is certain that one of the victim's associates played a part in his death. There's just one problem - since the DI had a direct view of the only staircase leading up to the room, he is the alibi for all the suspects. Meanwhile, JP worries about his marriage and seeks relationship advice from Dwayne

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Murdoch investigates when a string of vicious animal attacks in High Park terrorise the residents of Toronto.

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Brackenreid and some fellow inspectors unwind in a remote lodge, but their respite turns deadly when they find themselves hunted by a killer

programme.desc: Nabil Abdulrashid hosts the show filmed at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience before introducing stand-up routines by fellow comedians Jake Lambert and Rachel Fairburn

programme.title: Mel Giedroyc: Unforgivable

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Sean Kelly and the bidders are in Leamington Spa, and the up-market town brings out the businessman in Heavy D who gets more than he bargained for in one auction

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Things escalate between Nat and Boudicca and there are explosive consequences when Scotty fails to read a warning sign

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson road-tests the Jaguar XJR to see how it compares with the VW Phaeton, Richard Hammond recalls why the Mk2 was a landmark in motoring and the Aston Martin DB7 GT is pushed to the limit. Boris Johnson is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson looks at a range of Porsche 911s, James May gets behind the wheel of a classic Triumph TR6, and the small but powerful Clio V6 is pushed to the limit. Richard Hammond puts his case forward for the Land Rover as the greatest vehicle of all time, and Anne Robinson is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Motorhoming with Merton & Webster
programme.desc: Paul Merton and his wife Suki Webster hit the road as they embrace Britain's booming motorhoming scene, seeking out the UK's most famous sights and hidden gems, while there are tips from enthusiasts and volunteers test camping equipment. The couple begin their journey in Canterbury, where they pick up their cruiser and set off on their first-ever staycation on wheels touring Kent's coastline

programme.title: Turkey with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the presenter explores the country that lies at the crossroads between Europe and Asia, and discovers the dramatic changes it is undergoing. The first leg of his journey begins in Istanbul, from which he travels along the Aegean coast to the hostile border with Syria. He meets refugees trying to start a new life and a billionaire taking advantage of a property boom. He moves on into the Taurus Mountains and the Black Sea Coast, and visits a community trying to keep an ancient language alive

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Cameras follow old friends Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer as they embark on an angling trip round the UK. The first stop is Norfolk, and while fishing for tench, Bob tries to impress Paul with his childhood fishing rod - and fails. They share nostalgia for their younger years and reveal how they recently came face to face with their own mortality due to heart disease

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for barbel in Hay-on-Wye, and sample the great outdoors in spectacular fashion when Mortimer decides to strip in celebration of their catch. Passing a graveyard, they muse about the future and chat to a local vicar about death and their own funerals. To lighten the mood, back in their wooden fishing cabin, Bob promises Paul a very special treat if they are successful with their angling efforts

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Cameras follow old friends Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer as they continue on their angling trip round the UK. In the Monsal Valley in Derbyshire, Paul tries to teach Bob how to fly fish for wild rainbow trout, and the pair reflect on their humble beginnings, their careers and their comedy contemporaries. Later, Bob cooks Paul his very personal recipe of tuna melanie on the river bank, and it soon transpires his cooking is a lot more successful than his fishing

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Cameras follow old friends Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer as they continue on their angling trip round the UK. This time, Paul and Bob fish the river Wensum in Norfolk for roach, but as the pair struggle up and down the riverbanks, they begin to realise how age is starting to take its toll on their physicality. After staying overnight in a disused train station and visiting a local pub, the pair decide to challenge each other to a fishing competition the following day, where the winner gets a very special culinary treat

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: The pair fish at Christchurch's Bridge Pool in Dorset, hoping to catch sea trout, before Paul persuades Bob to charter a boat in search of sea bass. They venture out to the Needles where Paul makes a incredible catch, and decide to take the fish to a local restaurant to prepare it

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: In the final episode, Paul and Bob fish for Pike on the River Test in Hampshire, which may not be the best idea for two men of a certain age with heart problems. In the cottage where the pair are staying, they decide to invite a consultant cardiologist for dinner to talk about their medical past and their future, gaining tips on how to live the most healthy life possible. As the sun sets on the final night of their fishing expedition, Paul and Bob decide to write a eulogy for each on the riverbank

programme.title: Turkey with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the presenter explores the country that lies at the crossroads between Europe and Asia, and discovers the dramatic changes it is undergoing. The first leg of his journey begins in Istanbul, from which he travels along the Aegean coast to the hostile border with Syria. He meets refugees trying to start a new life and a billionaire taking advantage of a property boom. He moves on into the Taurus Mountains and the Black Sea Coast, and visits a community trying to keep an ancient language alive

programme.title: Turkey with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the second half of the journey, Simon travels along the Black Sea Coast to the capital of Ankara and the Northern Highlands, witnessing first hand the aftermath of fighting in Kurdish areas and meeting a conservationist attempting to preserve the region's brown bears. He then returns to Istanbul, where he takes part in a popular TV series set in the palace of the Ottoman Sultans

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers intercept an 18-wheeler carrying a load of narcotics into Canada, and banned fruit and veterinary medicines are found hidden in a pick-up truck

programme.title: Special Ops: Crime Squad UK
programme.desc: Special police task forces tackle a hit-and-run in Lancashire which turns out to be a deliberate attack on a single mum, and they also tackle a drug-dealing family dynasty in Bury

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Jo Brand hosts an extended episode of the satirical current affairs quiz, with team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton joined by comedian Nabil Abdulrashid and journalist Tom Peck

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks a completely random selection of questions this week, with Bill Bailey, Daliso Chaponda, Sally Phillips and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Host Dara O Briain and regular contributor Hugh Dennis bring the current series towards a close with a special edition of the topical comedy show featuring best bits and unseen material

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of fellow comedians Tom Allen, Angela Barnes, Rhys James, Nigel Ng and Athena Kugblenu. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Celebrity guests Carol Vorderman, Larry Lamb, Jo Brand and Russell Howard join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the comedy panel show in which contestants try to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Rob Brydon presents

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition. Comedian, novelist and presenter David Baddiel, comedian, actress and writer Cariad Lloyd and stand-up comedian and actor Ross Noble join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate questions and facts on the theme of nature and nurture. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult to crack that points are instead awarded for the answers deemed most interesting

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: In this special festive edition of the award-winning show, Tom Allen plays host in front of a packed audience and welcomes Thanyia Moore and Lloyd Griffith to the stage

programme.desc: Mel and Lou are joined by Mel Jamie Laing, Fern Brady and Jordan Stephens as Jamie and Jordan disclose their run-ins with the police

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A driver is stopped attempting to smuggle 15kg of cocaine across the border, and a truck driver's modified cab attracts attention at the World Trade Bridge

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A Cuban woman crosses the bridge in the hope of being granted political asylum in America, while officers in Laredo stop three students carrying something unusual in their car

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef embarks on a series of extended culinary breaks, visiting markets, restaurants, wineries, cafes and bars, looking for food excellence and also some innovative recipes that will encourage viewers to cook at home. He begins his first adventure in autumnal Bordeaux, where he helps judge a local wine contest and hires a 2CV for an excursion to the coast - abundant with mussels and oysters. At home, he cooks a memorable dish of steak frites with Bordelaise sauce

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef embarks on another round of extended culinary breaks, visiting markets, restaurants, wineries, cafes and bars, beginning with Portuguese capital Lisbon. The culinary effects of Portugal's historic explorations are still seen in its dishes with hints of cinnamon and freshly chopped coriander from the east and tomatoes and chillies from the Americas. Rick is inspired to cook salt cod fritters and an almond tart, before heading for the St Anthony's Day Parade and Sardine Festival

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond celebrates the Porsche 911 by taking the GT3 to its limits, James May tries out the BMW 5 series and Jeremy Clarkson tests Ford's supercar the GT40. Plus, a tribute to the endurance of the Toyota pickup, and a chance to see how the show's 20-year-old Jaguar finally met its end. Martin Kemp is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team visit the Isle of Man to drive the BMW Z4, Porsche Boxster and Honda S2000 on the famous TT circuit and Jeremy Clarkson puts the Mazda RX8 through its paces. Stephen Fry is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car and a Volvo estate is tested to its limits in an attempt to see how many caravans it can jump

Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Nabil Abdulrashid hosts the show filmed at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience before introducing stand-up routines by fellow comedians Jake Lambert and Rachel Fairburn

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition. Comedian, novelist and presenter David Baddiel, comedian, actress and writer Cariad Lloyd and stand-up comedian and actor Ross Noble join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate questions and facts on the theme of nature and nurture. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult to crack that points are instead awarded for the answers deemed most interesting

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: In this special festive edition of the award-winning show, Tom Allen plays host in front of a packed audience and welcomes Thanyia Moore and Lloyd Griffith to the stage

programme.title: Mel Giedroyc: Unforgivable
programme.desc: Mel and Lou are joined by Mel Jamie Laing, Fern Brady and Jordan Stephens as Jamie and Jordan disclose their run-ins with the police

programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A driver is stopped attempting to smuggle 15kg of cocaine across the border, and a truck driver's modified cab attracts attention at the World Trade Bridge

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A Cuban woman crosses the bridge in the hope of being granted political asylum in America, while officers in Laredo stop three students carrying something unusual in their car

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef embarks on a series of extended culinary breaks, visiting markets, restaurants, wineries, cafes and bars, looking for food excellence and also some innovative recipes that will encourage viewers to cook at home. He begins his first adventure in autumnal Bordeaux, where he helps judge a local wine contest and hires a 2CV for an excursion to the coast - abundant with mussels and oysters. At home, he cooks a memorable dish of steak frites with Bordelaise sauce

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef embarks on another round of extended culinary breaks, visiting markets, restaurants, wineries, cafes and bars, beginning with Portuguese capital Lisbon. The culinary effects of Portugal's historic explorations are still seen in its dishes with hints of cinnamon and freshly chopped coriander from the east and tomatoes and chillies from the Americas. Rick is inspired to cook salt cod fritters and an almond tart, before heading for the St Anthony's Day Parade and Sardine Festival

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond celebrates the Porsche 911 by taking the GT3 to its limits, James May tries out the BMW 5 series and Jeremy Clarkson tests Ford's supercar the GT40. Plus, a tribute to the endurance of the Toyota pickup, and a chance to see how the show's 20-year-old Jaguar finally met its end. Martin Kemp is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team visit the Isle of Man to drive the BMW Z4, Porsche Boxster and Honda S2000 on the famous TT circuit and Jeremy Clarkson puts the Mazda RX8 through its paces. Stephen Fry is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car and a Volvo estate is tested to its limits in an attempt to see how many caravans it can jump

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Cameras follow old friends Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer as they embark on an angling trip round the UK. The first stop is Norfolk, and while fishing for tench, Bob tries to impress Paul with his childhood fishing rod - and fails. They share nostalgia for their younger years and reveal how they recently came face to face with their own mortality due to heart disease

programme.title: Turkey with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the second half of the journey, Simon travels along the Black Sea Coast to the capital of Ankara and the Northern Highlands, witnessing first hand the aftermath of fighting in Kurdish areas and meeting a conservationist attempting to preserve the region's brown bears. He then returns to Istanbul, where he takes part in a popular TV series set in the palace of the Ottoman Sultans

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers reflect on the 1960s, a decade of music, fashion and food trends that are still around today. They prepare steak Diane and profiteroles, meet a restaurateur who made eating out trendy and look back on a time when the motorway cafe was the height of sophisticated dining for some

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: The group throws a party to celebrate Ann's first day in the department, while Leslie panics when she finds out that Ben has a meeting scheduled with her mother. Chris decides to implement some changes in the office, much to the dismay of Ron and Tom. Comedy, starring Amy Poehler and Rashida Jones

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: The success of Leslie's Li'l Sebastian Memorial gives rise to a new career opportunity for her, while Tom considers leaving the department. Comedy, starring Amy Poehler and Aziz Ansari

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Leslie struggles to hide her forbidden relationship with Ben, fearing it will threaten her chances of running for office in the future. Meanwhile, Ron deals with the arrival of his first wife, Tammy. Comedy starring Amy Poehler as an enthusiastic government official

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Shelagh organises a ballroom dance to raise money for the maternity home, in the hope of taking her mind off May's impending adoption. A terminally ill patient laments that she will not get the chance to dance one last time, but Sister Hilda is determined to make sure she does. Fred suffers an embarrassing condition, and cannot bring himself to see Dr Turner, while Nurse Crane is discharged from hospital and tries to play matchmaker between Sgt Woolf and Miss Higgins. Valerie and Trixie are called to testify in a court case

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: The United Britain beauty contest comes to Slaughter Abbey, and Miss Northern Ireland - one of the three favourites - is found dead in the swimming pool. Sister Boniface deduces murder from a blow to the back of the head. Drama, starring Lorna Watson

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Shepherd and his team investigate when Peggy Froome, a devoted fan of the '80s band Stolen Arrow, falls to her death from a bell tower. The detective must dig into the band's secrets and dark history to find the actual cause of the victim's demise. Neill Rea stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: Trust - A Sprat to Catch a Mackerel
programme.desc: Part one. A taxi driver provides a useful lead as the officers try to infiltrate a drugs deal involving Leroy Jones

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two. Unwitting bank robbers draft in undercover copper Rickman as their getaway driver _ but her involvement provokes an angry reaction from her superiors, who insist she's overstepped the mark and is getting out of control. Suzanne Maddock and Simon Rouse star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part three. Despite growing ever fonder, Rickman's trust in Leroy is tested to the limit as tensions between local gangs erupt into open warfare. Suzanne Maddock stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Boulton and Stanton's night in a luxury hotel goes horribly wrong when a series of robberies attracts the unwelcome attentions of DS Beech

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A softer side of Smith emerges after he helps deliver a baby. Alex Walkinshaw stars

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Shelagh organises a ballroom dance to raise money for the maternity home, in the hope of taking her mind off May's impending adoption. programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: After dealing with a power cut at the maternity home, Fred follows an unusual sound and finds a newborn baby abandoned in a dustbin. Meanwhile, Dr Turner and Nurse Crane must deal with an alarming outbreak of diphtheria, and Sister Julienne receives news of an ambitious redevelopment programme that potentially threatens Nonnatus House. Starring Jenny Agutter and Stephen McGann

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Girl
programme.desc: Feature-length period drama, starring Siobhan Flynn. A dying woman takes her daughter to the home of her former lover, a wealthy mine agent, who takes the child in. But the man's wife is less than happy about the arrangement and determines to keep the girl apart from her own offspring. With Jonathan Cake, Mark Benton and Susan Jameson

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: As Frances and Joyce battle for leadership of the new group, the others face problems of a much more personal nature. Erica and Will's relationship is tested after his cancer diagnosis, Pat is struggling with her unhappy marriage and Miriam has one last chance to stop David enlisting. Newcomer Teresa Fenchurch gets off to a bad start, but her mishap brings a dashing RAF officer to the village. Francesca Annis and Samantha Bond star, with Frances Grey, Ed Stoppard, Claire Rushbrook and Leanne Best

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The owners of a Tuscan inn are sent a severed finger and a photo of their son. Convinced he has been kidnapped by the Mafia, their daughter hires Hetty while she and Robert are on holiday

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Great Slaughter plays host to the summer roadshow of a popular children's TV programme Jolly Roger. Sister Boniface is called in to investigate when the editor is found murdered on set during the live show. Lorna Watson stars as the nun, moped rider, wine maker and part-time forensic scientist

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: A woman is murdered and the forensic evidence takes the team to a cosmetic clinic where the victim had been receiving various treatments seemingly for free, but Nikki and Silva are frustrated by the CEO of Hamilton Ashe hospital when he blocks access to Karen's medical records. Meanwhile, when his daughter Rosie turns up at the Lyell as her mum is in hospital over a pregnancy complication, Thomas must play the doting father for a few weeks - a role that does not come naturally

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The death of a teenage tennis star occupies the team when it is suggested her suicide jump two years earlier was anything but. They soon find several people with motives for her murder, including her coach Nick and agent Anthony, but it seems the one with the most to benefit from the girl's demise was her great rival Fawn Brammall - a shy and subdued player who proves difficult to question. Meanwhile, Gerry wonders if he did enough to encourage his own daughter's sporting ambitions and Brian hatches a plan to turn his dog into a movie star. Detective drama, guest starring Tamzin Outhwaite and Alexei Sayle

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: When popular local radio host Ryan Osbourne is killed in an explosion at his old high school, the shockwaves rattle the community as a fundraising initiative ends in tragedy. The hidden rivalries and secrets that emerge create a long list of suspects for Shepherd. Drama, starring Neill Rea

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Heston agrees to advance Daniel £1,000, then calls a meeting with the doctors to announce that he has hired Lily as the new partner and is reducing appointment times to five minutes, much to the dismay of everybody else. Meanwhile, George and Ronnie continue to argue about their holiday

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Dr Lily Hassan (Seeta Indrani) begins work at the Mill, and Heston decides to give her Daniel's consulting room. Her first case is a woman concerned about a recurring nightmare that her fiance is dead, and the situation is compounded when the man in question visits the surgery with chest pains

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: During a short-staffed bank holiday, Connie tries to persuade the board to ask for Zubin's resignation. Diane insists on working, despite feeling sick, but later collapses and is rushed into theatre - where she loses the baby. Ric tries to make peace with Jess, but a surprise revelation causes him to change his mind, and Mickie comes to a decision about her future. Former EastEnders actor Gary Beadle (Paul Trueman) guest stars as a man who saves his dead brother's wife from a burning building

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ruth helps a woman come to terms with her husband's terminal illness. Meanwhile, Dylan and Sam put aside their differences to diagnose a mystery condition and enable a teenage mother to keep her baby. Guest starring Susan Hampshire and Michael Jayston

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The stabbing of a woman in the street leads Klein to a Rwandan war criminal

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: As Peggy resumes ownership of the Vic, Phil continues to sink into oblivion. The fists and fireworks fly at Janine's hen do as Bianca and Ronnie go head to head

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: It is Janine and Ryan's wedding day, but will the day go without a hitch? Peggy ignores a captive Phil's pleas as she prepares for the Vic party

programme.title: Harry Wild
programme.desc: The Vibrant Villages competition is fierce, with dirty tricks and sabotage galore, but Harry's friend Evelyn Summers makes a far deadlier discovery

programme.title: The Doctor Blake Mysteries
programme.desc: Patrick Tyneman's son Edward, recently released from jail, is arrested for the murder of a freemason. Everyone suspects Edward, but Blake must put his former rivalry with the Tynemans aside as he investigates, with the help of his former police colleague Doug Ashby

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The police suspect Lovejoy of stealing from one of Jane's old friends, forcing him to enlist the help of refuse collector extraordinaire Brian Nun to clear himself. Unfortunately, matters are complicated by Eric's 'bin diving' habit. Light-hearted drama, starring Ian McShane and Phyllis Logan

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom becomes depressed after suffering a mild heart attack, so the management enlists Diana's help to try to cheer him up. Graham Crowden and Stephanie Cole star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Ivy receives an anonymous love letter and spends all her time trying to work out who sent it, little realising Compo, Clegg and Foggy already know. However, she soon rules out everyone except Smiler - and promptly resolves to put him straight once and for all

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard concocts a plan to allay Pearl's well-founded suspicions - he just needs to find someone as small as himself. Comedy, starring Bill Owen, Peter Sallis and Brian Wilde

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Smiler decides to provide a service to society by becoming a lollipop man, but unfortunately he does not start on good terms with the locals - a problem compounded by Foggy's decision to give him a crash course in aggression. Rural comedy, starring Stephen Lewis and Brian Wilde

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: A young, handsome and charismatic priest joins the parish, breathing new life into the church and boosting numbers - until a sudden death changes everything

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The death of a teenage tennis star occupies the team when it is suggested her suicide jump two years earlier was anything but. Detective drama, guest starring Tamzin Outhwaite and Alexei Sayle

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Tragedy strikes a leading football club when the team bus is hit by a train and the post-mortem examination reveals it was not an accident. Among the victims is manager Martin Bendelow, whose family has more than its fair share of skeletons in the cupboard, and more secrets are revealed when another murder takes place. Stephen Beckett guest stars, with Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan

programme.desc: Brokenwood is an unlikely beacon for extraterrestrial activity - until a local chef is found dead in a crop circle. Was it an alien abduction gone wrong?

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Vintage comedy featuring the much-loved duo performing sketches including Filling In, Martial Arts, Office Quickie and Vagabond Lover, plus a guest appearance by Elton John

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Vintage comedy from Messrs Barker and Corbett, with music by Elaine Paige, who sings Running Back for More

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Singer Elaine Paige performs If You Don't Want My Love in a musical sketch entitled Expensive House. Plus, The Band of Slaves continues and there's a Top of the Pops-themed finale

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: With guests Leslie Ash and Elaine Paige, and sketches including Coy George, Don't Quote Me, Roadsweepers, Raiders of the Last Auk and Bushman's Holiday

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Vintage comedy sketches featuring the much-loved duo, including Chef's Special, Humphrey and Godfrey, Boxing Day at the George and Campers. Guest Stephanie Lawrence provides the musical interlude

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Vintage comedy featuring the much-loved duo performing sketches including Filling In, Martial Arts, Office Quickie and Vagabond Lover, plus a guest appearance by Elton John

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: The actor is joined by his Uncle Matheson to test a night in a local youth hostel. They sign up for a cookery class, take a guided walk on Hadrian's Wall and visit remote honey bee hives

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Veteran actors Timothy West and Prunella Scales head to Llangollen, Denbighshire, where they spent their honeymoon. Reminiscing over how their relationship has survived and thrived over the past 50 years, the pair take in the picturesque sights of the Vale of Llangollen and the Montgomery Canal, before travelling on a horse-drawn barge up the Horseshoe Falls

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales visit the Burgundy region of France, where they travel along the Canal du Nivernais, which passes through towns and villages steeped in history. The duo also visit the market town of Chablis to help with the annual grape harvest and the medieval city of Auxerre before reflecting on what the journey has meant to them as a married couple

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales embark on another waterway adventure, starting in the romantic city of Oxford, where they began their courtship many years ago. They follow the gentle contours of the Oxford Canal through the Cherwell Valley, where they meet novelist Philip Pullman and are joined by their son, fellow actor Samuel West. Tim and Pru follow in the footsteps of author Tom Rolt, whose vivid account of his journey down this canal in 1939 ignited a campaign that went on to save Britain's canals from extinction

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Built nearly a century before the British canal system, the Canal du Midi in the south of France has been designated a world heritage site. Timothy West and Prunella Scales explore a picturesque stretch, as is it meanders toward the Mediterranean. They visit the town of Beziers and the Malpas Tunnel - Europe's first navigable canal tunnel - and taste the delights of lamb's brain fritters, as well as brandy that is almost 200 years old

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Veteran actors Timothy West and Prunella Scales head to Llangollen, Denbighshire, where they spent their honeymoon. Reminiscing over how their relationship has survived and thrived over the past 50 years, the pair take in the picturesque sights of the Vale of Llangollen and the Montgomery Canal, before travelling on a horse-drawn barge up the Horseshoe Falls

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Examining the heartland of the country, home to ancient structures such as the Great Wall and modern buildings like Beijing's Olympic Stadium, as well as animals including the giant panda and golden snub-nosed monkey. However, the region holds even deeper cultural significance as the land of origin of China's most populous ethnic group the Han people - whose 5,000-year-old traditions are increasingly under threat

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Built to shelter ancient China from hostile barbarians to the north, the Great Wall also serves as a cultural curtain, beyond which can be found a variety of traditions. This programme goes outside the Wall to meet tribes of reindeer herders on the Siberian border, and explores deserts to the north where ruined cities were once bustling staging posts for camel trains on the legendary Silk Road

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: The series concludes with a visit to the country's eastern seaboard to investigate how the Chinese government is managing environmental concerns while meeting the ever-expanding needs of the population. Among the region's diverse locales lie futuristic cities, seaweed-thatched villages and rugged wetlands that are home to some of the world's rarest animal species

programme.title: Wild Scandinavia
programme.desc: Rebecca Ferguson narrates a documentary exploring the wildlife, landscape and culture of the northern European region, beginning with a coastal journey from the sheltered Baltic Sea to the wild Atlantic Ocean, travelling north to freezing Arctic waters. Seal pups are born on granite islands left by the Ice Age, while flocks of migrant birds visit the largest tidal flats on the planet, and Europe's largest eagles patrol Norway's sea cliffs

programme.title: The Two Ronnies

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Vintage comedy featuring the much-loved duo performing sketches including Filling In, Martial Arts, Office Quickie and Vagabond Lover, plus a guest appearance by Elton John

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Veteran actors Timothy West and Prunella Scales head to Llangollen, Denbighshire, where they spent their honeymoon. Reminiscing over how their relationship has survived and thrived over the past 50 years, the pair take in the picturesque sights of the Vale of Llangollen and the Montgomery Canal, before travelling on a horse-drawn barge up the Horseshoe Falls

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales visit the Burgundy region of France, where they travel along the Canal du Nivernais, which passes through towns and villages steeped in history. The duo also visit the market town of Chablis to help with the annual grape harvest and the medieval city of Auxerre before reflecting on what the journey has meant to them as a married couple

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales embark on another waterway adventure, starting in the romantic city of Oxford, where they began their courtship many years ago. They follow the gentle contours of the Oxford Canal through the Cherwell Valley, where they meet novelist Philip Pullman and are joined by their son, fellow actor Samuel West. Tim and Pru follow in the footsteps of author Tom Rolt, whose vivid account of his journey down this canal in 1939 ignited a campaign that went on to save Britain's canals from extinction

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Built nearly a century before the British canal system, the Canal du Midi in the south of France has been designated a world heritage site. Timothy West and Prunella Scales explore a picturesque stretch, as is it meanders toward the Mediterranean. They visit the town of Beziers and the Malpas Tunnel - Europe's first navigable canal tunnel - and taste the delights of lamb's brain fritters, as well as brandy that is almost 200 years old

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Veteran actors Timothy West and Prunella Scales head to Llangollen, Denbighshire, where they spent their honeymoon. Reminiscing over how their relationship has survived and thrived over the past 50 years, the pair take in the picturesque sights of the Vale of Llangollen and the Montgomery Canal, before travelling on a horse-drawn barge up the Horseshoe Falls

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales visit the Burgundy region of France, where they travel along the Canal du Nivernais, which passes through towns and villages steeped in history. The duo also visit the market town of Chablis to help with the annual grape harvest and the medieval city of Auxerre before reflecting on what the journey has meant to them as a married couple

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Examining the heartland of the country, home to ancient structures such as the Great Wall and modern buildings like Beijing's Olympic Stadium, as well as animals including the giant panda and golden snub-nosed monkey. However, the region holds even deeper cultural significance as the land of origin of China's most populous ethnic group the Han people - whose 5,000-year-old traditions are increasingly under threat

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Built to shelter ancient China from hostile barbarians to the north, the Great Wall also serves as a cultural curtain, beyond which can be found a variety of traditions. This programme goes outside the Wall to meet tribes of reindeer herders on the Siberian border, and explores deserts to the north where ruined cities were once bustling staging posts for camel trains on the legendary Silk Road

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: The series concludes with a visit to the country's eastern seaboard to investigate how the Chinese government is managing environmental concerns while meeting the ever-expanding needs of the population. Among the region's diverse locales lie futuristic cities, seaweed-thatched villages and rugged wetlands that are home to some of the world's rarest animal species

programme.title: Wild Scandinavia
programme.desc: Rebecca Ferguson narrates a documentary exploring the wildlife, landscape and culture of the northern European region, beginning with a coastal journey from the sheltered Baltic Sea to the wild Atlantic Ocean, travelling north to freezing Arctic waters. Seal pups are born on granite islands left by the Ice Age, while flocks of migrant birds visit the largest tidal flats on the planet, and Europe's largest eagles patrol Norway's sea cliffs

programme.desc: More expertly crafted comedy sketches from Harry Enfield and colleagues-in-mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke, featuring the Camp Jockeys, the Scousers and Kevin the teenager

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mike and Alison welcome the very first guests to their new bijoux B&B. While serving the couple breakfast, Alison realises that they are not enjoying their break and, fearing a bad review, utilises the surprising skills of Lady Button and Julian to ensure the pair make the most of their weekend away. Thomas is perplexed yet secretly thrilled by the sudden attention from the Plaguers, who, after admiring Alison's discarded portrait of him are now affording him celebrity status in Button House, hanging on to his every word. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Geraldine rockets to stardom following an appearance on Terry Wogan's radio show, but finds fame something of a bugbear. Meanwhile, the Dibley Gala organisers prepare for a knockout performance. Guest starring former ballerina Darcey Bussell, with Dawn French and Gary Waldhorn

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del pays a stripper to attend a party at the Nag's Head with Rodney, who has been bet by Mickey Pearce that he cannot find a date for the occasion. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Uncle Albert has a nasty fall into the cellar at the Nag's Head, but turns his injury into a profit-making opportunity - eagerly egged on by wheeler-dealer Del-Boy. Vintage comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del and Rodney mourn Grandad's death, and feel gratified to see such a good turn-out of friends and relatives at his funeral - even if most people are more interested in the food afterwards. programme.desc: Del snatches a chance to make a quick profit, leaving gullible Denzil stripped of his redundancy money. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike receive some exciting news about their guesthouse business, but the fallout soon sees tensions running high between the pair. Pat and Thomas learn about the challenges of veganism, Kitty attempts to adopt a new way of communicating that sparks confusion between her and Alison. Lady Button is delighted by a new arrival that allows her to tap into a past passion. Robin however is less charmed by the new addition. Julian is confronted by a hard truth, but is determined to prove everyone wrong. Alison and Mike reach boiling point as Robin comes face to face with his biggest fear

The first episode of the sitcom, starring Richard Briers as an obsessive middle-aged man who yearns to be at the centre of his local suburban neighbourhood. With Penelope Wilton and Peter Egan. First broadcast in 1984

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher helps cellmate Godber make it through the night, calling on his unpredictable and limitless imagination to while away the hours. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Bryan welcomes his mail-order bride into his life - and his caravan - but there isn't enough room for her family as well. Llewellyn's student helpers disappear, so he hatches a plan to appropriate Boycie's staff and the new arrivals in one fell swoop. John Challis and Sue Holderness star

programme.desc: Old Mr Grace recognises a photo of Mr Humphries' mother and begins to suspect the head of menswear may be his son and heir. John Inman stars

programme.desc: Wally is called upon to settle an argument between Compo and Foggy - the exact size of Nora Batty's feet. Bill Owen, Kathy Staff and Joe Gladwin star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del seizes a rare opportunity to make a quick profit and bring Trotters Independent Traders into the hi-tech world of satellite communications. Meanwhile, Rodney plunges to the depths of despair as his marriage continues to deteriorate. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mike and Alison welcome the very first guests to their new bijoux B&B. While serving the couple breakfast, Alison realises that they are not enjoying their break and, fearing a bad review, utilises the surprising skills of Lady Button and Julian to ensure the pair make the most of their weekend away. Thomas is perplexed yet secretly thrilled by the sudden attention from the Plaguers, who, after admiring Alison's discarded portrait of him are now affording him celebrity status in Button House, hanging on to his every word. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Geraldine rockets to stardom following an appearance on Terry Wogan's radio show, but finds fame something of a bugbear. Meanwhile, the Dibley Gala organisers prepare for a knockout performance. Guest starring former ballerina Darcey Bussell, with Dawn French and Gary Waldhorn

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Janey visits her grandma and learns a disturbing family secret, while Ben is upset when Michael stands as a Conservative party candidate in the school election

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Susan tries to change her boss's unorthodox ideas about the tourist trade by inviting him to the family home for dinner - little expecting the turnaround it will inspire

programme.title: My Family
programme.sub-title: Ben Wants to Be a Millionaire
programme.desc: Ben feels a touch of envy when he meets up with an old school friend who is now a millionaire - but remembers how he never liked him in the first place

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Nick's latest hare-brained career plan lands him in hospital, while Janey's scheme to get a job as a waitress, to be recognised and catapulted to fame, falls flat when she fails to spot a single celebrity in the restaurant

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Janey finally leaves home for university, leaving the rest of the family squabbling over who gets custody of her bedroom. Nick cleans up his act and lands a job at the local supermarket, but once he gets his hands on a pricing gun it seems nothing is sacred. Domestic comedy, starring Robert Lindsay, Zoe Wanamaker and Kris Marshall

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Senior neurologist Dr Colin Driver comes up with an unconventional solution to treat a nurse's cat, which has been rushed into the surgery after being poisoned. The owners of a rare Polish sheepdog with a failed fracture repair on a broken leg are faced with the option of having the leg removed, or trying an innovative total elbow replacement. Plus, a self-confessed 'crazy cat lady' brings in a ginger tom which urgently needs leg surgery

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Staffordshire bull terrier requires attention when it suffers complete paralysis in its back legs, and a Devon rex cat is brought in after being struck by a car. Its eight-year-old owner faces a tough decision when X-rays reveal a severely broken pelvis and a ruptured bladder. Plus, a young cavalier king has had seven operations in a bid to fix its knee joint, and Noel tries to resolve the problem

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick pushes the boundaries of veterinary medicine, and treats a German shepherd puppy that needs surgery on his hips and front leg using groundbreaking cell treatment. The vet must also perform delicate surgery on a chihuahua suffering from a neck deformity compressing its spinal cord - however, one false move could be fatal. Plus, a rescue dog with deformed front legs is brought into the surgery

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Festive edition of the documentary from 2015 following the work of Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team. A sleigh-pulling Alaskan malamute needs both knees replacing with implants, but his owners are forced to make a difficult decision as he recovers from his first operation. Meanwhile, a springer spaniel is rushed into the surgery with a shattered jaw, so the vets decide to try something new to get him home in time for Christmas. Plus, a miniature dachshund with a deformed foot is given intensive physiotherapy, and Noel gets a surprise visitor

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Senior neurologist Dr Colin Driver comes up with an unconventional solution to treat a nurse's cat, which has been rushed into the surgery after being poisoned. The owners of a rare Polish sheepdog with a failed fracture repair on a broken leg are faced with the option of having the leg removed, or trying an innovative total elbow replacement. Plus, a self-confessed 'crazy cat lady' brings in a ginger tom which urgently needs leg surgery

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Staffordshire bull terrier requires attention when it suffers complete paralysis in its back legs, and a Devon rex cat is brought in after being struck by a car. Its eight-year-old owner faces a tough decision when X-rays reveal a severely broken pelvis and a ruptured bladder. Plus, a young cavalier king has had seven operations in a bid to fix its knee joint, and Noel tries to resolve the problem

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick pushes the boundaries of veterinary medicine, and treats a German shepherd puppy that needs surgery on his hips and front leg using groundbreaking cell treatment. The vet must also perform delicate surgery on a chihuahua suffering from a neck deformity compressing its spinal cord - however, one false move could be fatal. Plus, a rescue dog with deformed front legs is brought into the surgery

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Festive edition of the documentary from 2015 following the work of Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team. A sleigh-pulling Alaskan malamute needs both knees replacing with implants, but his owners are forced to make a difficult decision as he recovers from his first operation. Meanwhile, a springer spaniel is rushed into the surgery with a shattered jaw, so the vets decide to try something new to get him home in time for Christmas. programme.desc: Countdown's Rachel Riley and her husband and former Strictly partner Pasha Kovalev compete, teaming up with Paul Martin and David Harper to help scour the shops of Essex. programme.title: I Am Ali
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the life story of Muhammad Ali. The film features unprecedented access to the heavyweight champion's personal archives and journals, and features interviews and testimonials with family and friends, as well as contributions from fellow boxers including Mike Tyson, George Foreman and Gene Kilroy

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Sunderland to pick up a real Italian beauty - a 1972 Lancia Fulvia Zagato. Red and racy, the car has seen better days, but Derek's still impressed

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Up for sale at the Mathewson's auction - a 1275 Mini GT that's come from just down the road that could be a future British icon

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek goes to pick up a veteran 1904 Swift, there's an emotional farewell to a 1980s Bedford HA van, and a Toyota Corona finds a new home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Mega Metro
programme.desc: An extension is now underway to get people into the centre of Australia's biggest city Sydney, including a new tunnel-crossing deep under the harbour

programme.title: Mega Metro
programme.desc: We go back under Sydney Harbour to check out history in the making as the construction of the twin metro rail tunnels continues deep under the surface

programme.title: Great Indian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Guided by his Bradshaw's 1913 Handbook of Indian, Foreign and Colonial Travel, Michael Portillo embarks on a rail journey from Amritsar to Shimla. Along the way he helps to feed people at the world's largest free kitchen, and travels the railway routes used by millions of migrants during Partition. Beginning in the Sikh holy city, Michael is dazzled by the beauty of the Golden Temple and awed by the scale of its langar - the world's largest free kitchen. His route then takes him through the Punjab, and at Kalka, Michael glimpses the Himalayas for the first time

programme.title: Great Indian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Jodhpur to Delhi, heading into the desert to take a camel ride to a village where life has changed little in centuries, before embarking on the Jaipur-Agra-Delhi 'Golden Triangle' tour - India's must-do itinerary in 1913 as today. Michael also visits Agra, where he learns how the Taj Mahal, built by a Mughal emperor, was restored by a British viceroy at the time of his Bradshaw's guide book

programme.title: Great Indian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's Bradshaw's 1913 Handbook of Indian, Foreign and Colonial Travel leads him on railway journey through the modern south Indian states of Karnataka and Tamil Nadu. He travels from the former princely state of Mysuru, aka Mysore, to the first stronghold of the East India Company in Chennai, formerly Madras

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: As Robbie Cumming leaves the scenic Llangollen Canal, he faces a journey of running repairs and narrowboat maintenance on the old Naughty Lass

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: With the Anderton Boat Lift out of action, Robbie Cumming explores the scenic River Weaver onboard a borrowed narrowboat called 'Tench'

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie navigates the Oxford Canal, bumps into a hire boat and a hedge and tackles large river locks on the scenic Upper River Thames

programme.title: Sacred Rivers with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer travels to China for a trip along the Yangtze, Asia's longest river and the third longest in the world. He follows the route to Dazu, where he sees 50,000 ancient rock carvings, some of the only religious artefacts not to have been destroyed under communist rule. He then takes a river cruise to the Three Gorges Dam - the biggest power station on the planet - before ending his journey in Shanghai, a shining symbol of China's new economic might and a city where many are rediscovering religious faith

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the explorer travels through Cornwall as its emerges from the first lockdown, discovering the impact of Covid-19 on a part of the country highly reliant on tourism. Simon discovers the ongoing impact of the collapse of the tin-mining industry in the 1980s when he visits one of Britain's poorest estates, and meets the entrepreneurs who sell homemade food on the beach who are struggling to make enough money to last the winter

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the conclusion of his two-part journey, Simon investigates the environmental challenges facing Cornwall and the rest of the country. He discovers how the region's farmers are adopting new, less intensive approaches that are both more profitable and environmentally friendly, and reveals how reintroducing beavers to the uplands could tackle the problem of widespread flooding. He also encounters some of the county's iconic wildlife, including grey seals that live in constant risk of getting caught up in discarded fishing nets

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie returns to where his boating life began, exploring London's canals on Naughty Lass. He travels through Little Venice and moors in Paddington Basin

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: As Robbie Cumming leaves the scenic Llangollen Canal, he faces a journey of running repairs and narrowboat maintenance on the old Naughty Lass

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries

programme.title: Mega Metro
programme.desc: An extension is now underway to get people into the centre of Australia's biggest city Sydney, including a new tunnel-crossing deep under the harbour

programme.title: Mega Metro
programme.desc: We go back under Sydney Harbour to check out history in the making as the construction of the twin metro rail tunnels continues deep under the surface

programme.title: Great Indian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Guided by his Bradshaw's 1913 Handbook of Indian, Foreign and Colonial Travel, Michael Portillo embarks on a rail journey from Amritsar to Shimla. Along the way he helps to feed people at the world's largest free kitchen, and travels the railway routes used by millions of migrants during Partition. Beginning in the Sikh holy city, Michael is dazzled by the beauty of the Golden Temple and awed by the scale of its langar - the world's largest free kitchen. His route then takes him through the Punjab, and at Kalka, Michael glimpses the Himalayas for the first time

programme.title: Great Indian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Jodhpur to Delhi, heading into the desert to take a camel ride to a village where life has changed little in centuries, before embarking on the Jaipur-Agra-Delhi 'Golden Triangle' tour - India's must-do itinerary in 1913 as today. Michael also visits Agra, where he learns how the Taj Mahal, built by a Mughal emperor, was restored by a British viceroy at the time of his Bradshaw's guide book

programme.title: Great Indian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's Bradshaw's 1913 Handbook of Indian, Foreign and Colonial Travel leads him on railway journey through the modern south Indian states of Karnataka and Tamil Nadu. He travels from the former princely state of Mysuru, aka Mysore, to the first stronghold of the East India Company in Chennai, formerly Madras

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: As Robbie Cumming leaves the scenic Llangollen Canal, he faces a journey of running repairs and narrowboat maintenance on the old Naughty Lass

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: With the Anderton Boat Lift out of action, Robbie Cumming explores the scenic River Weaver onboard a borrowed narrowboat called 'Tench'

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie navigates the Oxford Canal, bumps into a hire boat and a hedge and tackles large river locks on the scenic Upper River Thames

programme.title: Sacred Rivers with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer travels to China for a trip along the Yangtze, Asia's longest river and the third longest in the world. He follows the route to Dazu, where he sees 50,000 ancient rock carvings, some of the only religious artefacts not to have been destroyed under communist rule. He then takes a river cruise to the Three Gorges Dam - the biggest power station on the planet - before ending his journey in Shanghai, a shining symbol of China's new economic might and a city where many are rediscovering religious faith

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the explorer travels through Cornwall as its emerges from the first lockdown, discovering the impact of Covid-19 on a part of the country highly reliant on tourism. Simon discovers the ongoing impact of the collapse of the tin-mining industry in the 1980s when he visits one of Britain's poorest estates, and meets the entrepreneurs who sell homemade food on the beach who are struggling to make enough money to last the winter

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the conclusion of his two-part journey, Simon investigates the environmental challenges facing Cornwall and the rest of the country. He discovers how the region's farmers are adopting new, less intensive approaches that are both more profitable and environmentally friendly, and reveals how reintroducing beavers to the uplands could tackle the problem of widespread flooding. He also encounters some of the county's iconic wildlife, including grey seals that live in constant risk of getting caught up in discarded fishing nets

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie returns to where his boating life began, exploring London's canals on Naughty Lass. He travels through Little Venice and moors in Paddington Basin

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.sub-title: The Green Man Pub and Hotel, Wembley
programme.desc: The Green Man Pub and Hotel is in a prime location near Wembley Stadium, but bad reviews and old-fashioned bedrooms are putting off guests and forcing owner Mel Preedy to rely on the establishment's pub to support the hotel side of the business. Alex Polizzi heads to north London to attempt to reverse Mel's fortunes

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part two of two. The former Republic of Ireland manager opens up about life as a manager, taking the Ireland job twice, the 2002 World Cup, and the infamous relationship with Roy Keane

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the former football player and manager, who talks to Tommy Martin about his life as well as his sporting career

programme.title: Building Giants
programme.desc: The race to build a massive new rollercoaster in Canada's biggest theme park before it opens its gates for the summer - a ride with a record-breaking 90-degree vertical drop

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three single women battle to win the affections of a Black Country barber Josh, hoping their style choices will bag them a date

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Stripper Darren hopes to be impressed by the style choices of three potential suitors, Sade, Shannon and Kalisha

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: In Somerset, Ruth Watson visits the three-diamond, eight-bedroom hotel Saxonia in Weston-super-Mare. She suggests to owners Jon and Sandie Harrap that unattractive decor and cluttered, kitschy rooms could explain why customers are no longer flocking to stay

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines real-life cases, looking at the events leading up to crimes, going through the police investigations and summing up the trials

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Documentary following life in the Spanish resort, this time at the Hotel Benidorm Plaza as Easter sees the start of the new holiday season. Checking in this week is a female rugby team from Manchester looking to let off some steam, a couple returning to relive their honeymoon, and friends hoping for solace in the sun

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: As the team at the Hotel Benidorm Plaza prepare to throw a party to show off the hotel to local travel agents, the Spanish resort is hit by a rare rainstorm, resulting in the hotel springing leaks and causing the electricity to cut out. Meanwhile, a 14-strong friendship group party in memory of their dear friend, and a couple celebrate their 25th wedding anniversary at Benidorm's famously romantic waterfalls

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade takes on Africa's wild Zambezi River to find an unknown predator dragging fishermen to their deaths. Crocodiles and hippos are guilty of killing dozens of victims in plain view, yet it is said there is another monstrous beast stalking these deep and turbulent waters. Locals speak of 'the Mazunda' - but what is this sinister, child-swallowing creature?

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Presenter Helen Skelton and expert garden designer Danny Clarke bring unloved gardens back to life in a single day using clever design, imaginative ideas and recycling or re-use of existing plants and materials. They begin by travelling to Blackpool to transform Paul and Sarah's boggy, waterlogged plot
